text: AWS allows users to manage their resources using a web based user interface. What is the name of this interface?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: The AWS Management Console is a web-based user interface that allows users to manage their AWS resources. It provides a graphical interface for most AWS services.

text: Which of the following is an example of horizontal scaling in the AWS Cloud?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Horizontal scaling, also known as scaling out, involves adding more instances to your pool of resources. This distributes the load across multiple machines, unlike vertical scaling which increases the capacity of a single machine.

answers.text: Replacing an existing EC2 instance with a larger, more powerful one.
Increasing the compute capacity of a single EC< instance to address the growing demands of an application.
Adding more RAM capacity to an EC2 instance.
Adding more EC2 instances of the same size to handle an increase in traffic.

text: You have noticed that several critical Amazon EC2 instances have been terminated. Which of the following AWS services would help you determine who took this action?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail records API calls made in your AWS account, including those that terminate EC2 instances. This allows you to identify the user or role responsible for the action.

text: Which of the below options are related to the reliability of AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Reliability in AWS is achieved through automatic resource provisioning to meet demand and the ability to recover quickly from failures. These aspects align with the AWS Well-Architected Framework's reliability pillar.

answers.text: Applying the principle of least privilege to all AWS resources.
Automatically provisioning new resources to meet demand.
All AWS services are considered Global Services, and this design helps customers serve their international users.
Providing compensation to customers if issues occur.
Ability to recover quickly from failures.

text: Which statement is true regarding the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?
explanation: The AWS Shared Responsibility Model states that AWS is responsible for the security OF the cloud, while the customer is responsible for security IN the cloud. The specific division of responsibilities varies depending on the service being used by the customer.

answers.text: Responsibilities vary depending on the services used.
Security of the IaaS services is the responsibility of AWS.
Patching the guest OS is always the responsibility of AWS.
Security of the managed services is the responsibility of the customer.

text: You have set up consolidated billing for several AWS accounts. One of the accounts has purchased a number of reserved instances for 3 years. Which of the following is true regarding this scenario?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: In AWS consolidated billing, Reserved Instance (RI) discounts are applied to the usage across all accounts within the organization. Therefore, all accounts can receive the hourly cost benefit of the Reserved Instances purchased by any account in the consolidated bill group.

answers.text: The Reserved Instance discounts can only be shared with the master account.
All accounts can receive the hourly cost benefit of the Reserved Instances.
The purchased instances will have better performance than On-demand instances.
There are no cost benefits from using consolidated billing; It is for informational purposes only.

text: A company has developed an eCommerce web application in AWS. What should they do to ensure that the application has the highest level of availability?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Deploying across multiple AWS Regions provides the highest level of availability by protecting against large-scale regional outages. Within each Region, using multiple Availability Zones ensures resilience against single data center failures.

answers.text: Deploy the application across multiple Availability Zones and Edge locations.
Deploy the application across multiple Availability Zones and subnets.
Deploy the application across multiple Regions and Availability Zones.
Deploy the application across multiple VPC's and subnets.

text: What does AWS Snowball provide? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Snowball offers built-in computing capabilities for local data processing and provides a secure method for transferring large volumes of data into and out of AWS.

answers.text: Built-in computing capabilities that allow customers to process data locally.
A catalog of third-party software solutions that customers need to build solutions and run their businesses.
A hybrid cloud storage between on-premises environments and the AWS Cloud.
An Exabyte-scale data transfer service that allows you to move extremely large amounts of data to AWS.
Secure transfer of large amounts of data into and out of the AWS.

text: A company has an AWS Enterprise Support plan. They want quick and efficient guidance with their billing and account inquiries. Which of the following should the company use?
explanation: AWS Support Concierge is a benefit of the Enterprise Support plan, designed to provide quick and efficient guidance for billing and account inquiries.

text: A Japanese company hosts their applications on Amazon EC2 instances in the Tokyo Region. The company has opened new branches in the United States, and the US users are complaining of high latency. What can the company do to reduce latency for the users in the US while minimizing costs?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Deploying new Amazon EC2 instances in a Region located in the US will place resources closer to US users, thereby reducing latency. This is a cost-effective solution compared to building a new data center or complex routing configurations.

answers.text: Applying the Amazon Connect latency-based routing policy.
Registering a new US domain name to serve the users in the US.
Building a new data center in the US and implementing a hybrid model.
Deploying new Amazon EC2 instances in a Region located in the US.

text: An organization has a large number of technical employees who operate their AWS Cloud infrastructure. What does AWS provide to help organize them into teams and then assign the appropriate permissions for each team?
explanation: AWS IAM user groups allow you to organize IAM users into logical collections, simplifying the management of permissions by assigning policies to the group rather than to individual users.

text: A company has decided to migrate its Oracle database to AWS. Which AWS service can help achieve this without negatively impacting the functionality of the source database?
explanation: AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) facilitates the migration of databases to AWS, supporting various source and target databases, including Oracle. It enables homogenous and heterogeneous database migrations, minimizing downtime and impact on the source database by performing replication.

text: Adjusting compute capacity dynamically to reduce cost is an implementation of which AWS cloud best practice?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Implementing elasticity involves dynamically adjusting compute capacity to match demand, which helps in optimizing costs by scaling resources up or down as needed.

text: What are the benefits of having infrastructure hosted in AWS? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS provides unmatched speed and agility by allowing you to provision resources quickly, and it handles much of the physical security and data/network security through the shared responsibility model.

answers.text: There is no need to worry about security.
Gaining complete control over the physical infrastructure.
Operating applications on behalf of customers.
All of the physical security and most of the data/network security are taken care of for you.

text: What is the advantage of the AWS-recommended practice of 'decoupling' applications?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Decoupling applications reduces inter-dependencies between components. This means that a failure in one component is less likely to affect other parts of the application, improving overall resilience.

answers.text: Allows treating an application as a single, cohesive unit.
Reduces inter-dependencies so that failures do not impact other components of the application.
Allows updates of any monolithic application quickly and easily.
Allows tracking of any API call made to any AWS service.

text: Which of the following helps a customer view the Amazon EC2 billing activity for the past month?
explanation: AWS Cost & Usage Reports provide granular data about AWS costs and usage, allowing customers to view detailed billing activity for services like Amazon EC2 over a past month.

text: What do you gain from setting up consolidated billing for five different AWS accounts under another master account?
explanation: Consolidated billing allows multiple AWS accounts to receive volume discounts as their combined usage increases. This means that while individual accounts don't get separate discounts, the aggregated usage can lower the overall cost for all accounts.

answers.text: AWS services' costs will be reduced to half the original price.
The consolidated billing feature is just for organizational purpose.
Each AWS account gets volume discounts.
Each AWS account gets five times the free-tier services capacity.

text: What should you do in order to keep the data on EBS volumes safe? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Creating EBS snapshots allows you to back up your EBS volumes at a point in time, and encrypting EBS data at rest ensures that even if the underlying storage is compromised, the data remains unreadable.

answers.text: Regularly update firmware on EBS devices.
Ensure that EBS data is encrypted at rest.
Store a backup daily in an external drive.
Prevent any unauthorized access to AWS data centers.

text: One of the most important AWS best-practices to follow is the cloud architecture principle of elasticity. How does this principle improve your architecture's design?
explanation: Elasticity in the AWS Cloud refers to the ability to automatically provision the right amount of AWS resources to meet changes in demand. This ensures optimal performance and cost-efficiency by scaling up or down as needed.

answers.text: By automatically scaling your on-premises resources based on changes in demand.
By automatically scaling your AWS resources using an Elastic Load Balancer.
By reducing interdependencies between application components wherever possible.
By automatically provisioning the required AWS resources based on changes in demand.

text: A startup company is operating on limited funds and is extremely concerned about cost overruns. Which of the below options can be used to notify the company when their monthly AWS bill exceeds $2000? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Budgets allows you to set custom cost and usage budgets and receive alerts when actual or forecasted costs and usage exceed (or are forecasted to exceed) your budgeted amount. CloudWatch billing alarms can also be configured to trigger SNS notifications when cost thresholds are met.

answers.text: Setup a CloudWatch billing alarm that triggers an SNS notification when the threshold is exceeded.
Configure the Amazon Simple Email Service to send billing alerts to their email address on a daily basis.
Configure the AWS Budgets Service to alert the company when the threshold is exceeded.
Configure AWS CloudTrail to automatically delete all AWS resources when the threshold is exceeded.
Configure the Amazon Connect Service to alert the company when the threshold is exceeded.

text: What does Amazon CloudFront use to distribute content to global users with low latency?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a global content delivery network (CDN) that caches content at edge locations worldwide to deliver it to end-users with low latency. AWS Regions and Availability Zones are datacenter locations, while AWS Global Accelerator optimizes network traffic but is not the primary distribution mechanism for CloudFront.

text: What does the 'Principle of Least Privilege' refer to?
explanation: The Principle of Least Privilege dictates that users should only be granted the minimum permissions necessary to perform their tasks. This minimizes the potential impact of compromised credentials or accidental misuse.

answers.text: You should grant your users only the permissions they need when they need them and nothing more.
AllIAM users should have at least the necessary permissions to access the core AWS services.
All trusted IAM users should have access to any AWS service in the respective AWS account.
IAM users should not be granted any permissions; to keep your account safe.

text: Which of the following does NOT belong to the AWS Cloud Computing models?
explanation: The three main cloud computing service models are Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service (PaaS), and Software as a Service (SaaS). Networking as a Service (NaaS) is not one of the primary, widely recognized cloud computing models.

text: The identification process of an online financial services company requires that new users must complete an online interview with their security team. The completed recorded interviews are only required in the event of a legal issue or a regulatory compliance breach. What is the most cost-effective service to store the recorded videos?
explanation: Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the most cost-effective storage option for data that is accessed very rarely, such as recorded videos that are only needed in the event of a legal issue or compliance breach.

text: Which service provides DNS in the AWS cloud?
explanation: Amazon Route 53 is a highly available and scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service. It provides a fully automated way to Route end users to an application. AWS Config is used for configuration management, Amazon CloudFront is a CDN, and Amazon EMR is for big data processing.

text: Hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks are recorded every month worldwide. What service does AWS provide to help protect AWS Customers from these attacks? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Shield protects against DDoS attacks by providing always-on detection and automatic inline mitigations that block attacks. AWS WAF is a web application firewall that helps protect web applications or APIs against common web exploits that could affect application availability, compromise security, or consume excessive resources, including some types of DDoS attacks.

text: A company is deploying a new two-tier web application in AWS. Where should the most frequently accessed data be stored so that the application's response time is optimal?
explanation: Amazon ElastiCache is an in-memory data store and cache service that can significantly improve application response times by storing frequently accessed data. While an Amazon EBS volume provides block-level storage for EC2 instances, it is a persistent storage solution and not optimized for low-latency, in-memory caching.

text: You want to run a questionnaire application for only one day (without interruption), which Amazon EC2 purchase option should you use?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: On-Demand Instances are ideal for applications with short-term, spiky, or unpredictable workloads that cannot be interrupted, making them suitable for a one-day questionnaire application.

text: You are working on a project that involves creating thumbnails of millions of images. Consistent uptime is not an issue, and continuous processing is not required. Which EC2 buying option would be the most cost-effective?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Spot Instances offer the largest savings compared to On-Demand prices, making them ideal for fault-tolerant workloads with flexible start and end times like batch image processing.

text: Which of the following can be described as a global content delivery network (CDN) service?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a global content delivery network (CDN) service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers with low latency and high transfer speeds.

text: Which of the following services allows customers to manage their agreements with AWS?
explanation: AWS Artifact provides a single place to access AWS compliance reports and agreements, helping customers manage their compliance. AWS Certificate Manager manages SSL/TLS certificates, AWS Systems Manager manages operational tasks, and AWS Organizations manages multiple AWS accounts.

text: Which of the following are examples of AWS-Managed Services, where AWS is responsible for the operational and maintenance burdens of running the service? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS-Managed Services are those where AWS handles the underlying infrastructure, patching, and maintenance. Amazon DynamoDB and Amazon EMR are examples of services where AWS manages the operational burden.

text: Your company has a data store application that requires access to a NoSQL database. Which AWS database offering would meet this requirement?
explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service that provides fast performance at any scale, making it suitable for applications requiring NoSQL capabilities.

text: As part of the Enterprise support plan, who is the primary point of contact for ongoing support needs?
explanation: Technical Account Managers (TAMs) are assigned to Enterprise Support customers and serve as their primary point of contact for proactive guidance and ongoing support needs.

answers.text: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user.

text: How can you view the distribution of AWS spending in one of your AWS accounts?
explanation: AWS Cost Explorer provides a powerful tool to visualize, understand, and manage your AWS costs and usage over time. It allows you to filter and group your spending data to identify trends and optimize costs.

answers.text: By contacting the AWS Support team.
By contacting the AWS Finance team.

text: Which of the following must an IAM user provide to interact with AWS services using the AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI)?
explanation: IAM users must generate access keys, which consist of an access key ID and a secret access key, to authenticate and authorize their requests when using the AWS CLI or AWS SDKs.

text: You have AWS Basic support, and you have discovered that some AWS resources are being used maliciously, and those resources could potentially compromise your data. What should you do?
explanation: The AWS Abuse team handles reports of malicious activity on AWS. Contacting them is the appropriate action when discovering compromised or maliciously used resources.

answers.text: Contact the AWS Customer Service team.

text: Select TWO examples of the AWS shared controls.
explanation: AWS shared controls are security and compliance responsibilities that apply to both AWS and its customers. Patch Management and Configuration Management are examples of these shared responsibilities.

text: In order to implement best practices when dealing with a 'Single Point of Failure,' you should attempt to build as much automation as possible in both detecting and reacting to failure. Which of the following AWS services would help? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Auto Scaling automates the process of scaling your compute resources up or down based on demand, and Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets in multiple Availability Zones, helping to eliminate single points of failure.

text: A company is planning to host an educational website on AWS. Their video courses will be streamed all around the world. Which of the following AWS services will help achieve high transfer speeds?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a content delivery network (CDN) that caches content closer to users worldwide, significantly improving transfer speeds for streaming video.

text: A developer is planning to build a two-tier web application that has a MySQL database layer. Which of the following AWS database services would provide automated backups for the application?
explanation: Amazon Aurora automatically performs and stores backups of your database, making it easier to recover your data in case of failures or accidental deletions. While a MySQL database on EC2 could be configured for backups, it requires manual setup and management.

answers.text: A MySQL database installed on an EC2 instance.

text: What is the AWS service that enables AWS architects to manage infrastructure as code?
explanation: AWS CloudFormation is the service that allows architects to model and provision AWS resources using a declarative language, effectively managing infrastructure as code.

text: Under the shared responsibility model, which of the following is the responsibility of AWS?
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security OF the cloud, which includes the physical infrastructure and global network. Configuring infrastructure devices falls under this purview. The customer is responsible for security IN the cloud, which includes client-side encryption, server-side encryption, and traffic filtering with Security Groups.

text: What does the AWS Health Dashboard provide? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The AWS Health Dashboard provides a personalized view of AWS service health and a dashboard detailing vulnerabilities in your applications and their potential impact on your AWS resources. It also offers detailed troubleshooting guidance for AWS events impacting your resources.

answers.text: Detailed troubleshooting guidance to address AWS events impacting your resources.
Health checks for Auto Scaling instances.
A dashboard detailing vulnerabilities in your applications.
Personalized view of AWS service health.

text: You have deployed your application on multiple Amazon EC2 instances. Your customers complain that sometimes they can't reach your application. Which AWS service allows you to monitor the performance of your EC2 instances to assist in troubleshooting these issues?
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is used to monitor AWS resources and applications in real time, including the performance of EC2 instances. AWS Lambda is a compute service, AWS Config tracks resource configuration, and AWS CloudTrail logs API activity.

text: Your company is developing a critical web application in AWS, and the security of the application is a top priority. Which of the following AWS services will provide infrastructure security optimization recommendations?
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor provides infrastructure security optimization recommendations. It checks for security issues and suggests improvements across various pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework, including security.

text: Which of the following is not a benefit of Amazon S3? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Amazon S3 provides virtually unlimited storage capacity and durability, and while it scales automatically, it does not require manual scaling. It is primarily an object storage service and cannot run applications or backend systems directly.

answers.text: Amazon S3 provides unlimited storage for any type of data.
Amazon S3 can run any type of application or backend system.
Amazon S3 stores any number of objects, but with object size limits.
Amazon S3 can be scaled manually to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere.
Amazon S3 provides 99.999999999% (11 9's) of data durability.

text: In the AWS Shared responsibility Model, which of the following are the responsibility of the customer? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Customers are responsible for configuring network access rules and setting password complexity rules as part of the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, which ensures security at every layer of the cloud infrastructure.

answers.text: Controlling physical access to compute resources.

text: What does AWS provide to deploy popular technologies such as IBM MQ on AWS with the least amount of effort and time?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS Quick Starts are automated reference deployments that help you deploy popular technologies on AWS quickly and easily. They are built by AWS and its partners to help customers provision applications and infrastructure on AWS without manual configuration.

text: An organization has decided to purchase an Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance (RI) for three years in order to reduce costs. It is possible that the application workloads could change during the reservation period. What is the EC2 Reserved Instance (RI) type that will allow the company to exchange the purchased reserved instance for another reserved instance with higher computing power if they need to?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Convertible Reserved Instances allow you to exchange your current RI for another RI with different attributes, such as instance type, operating system, or tenancy, to accommodate changes in your application workload.

text: A global company with a large number of AWS accounts is seeking a way in which they can centrally manage billing and security policies across all accounts. Which AWS Service will assist them in meeting these goals?
explanation: AWS Organizations allows you to centrally manage and govern your environment as you grow and scale your AWS Cloud adoption. It helps you control access to AWS services and resources for each AWS account, automate account creation, and consolidate billing. AWS Config and AWS Trusted Advisor support governance and security but do not offer the centralized account management for billing.

text: Which service provides object-level storage in AWS?
explanation: Amazon S3 is AWS's object storage service, designed for high scalability, data availability, security, and performance. Amazon EBS provides block storage for EC2 instances, and Amazon EFS provides scalable file storage.

text: A company is concerned that they are spending money on underutilized compute resources in AWS. Which AWS feature will help ensure that their applications are automatically adding/removing EC2 compute capacity to closely match the required demand?
explanation: AWS Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the number of Amazon EC2 instances in response to changing demand, helping to maintain application performance and reduce costs associated with over-provisioning.

text: Which S3 storage class is best for data with unpredictable access patterns?
explanation: Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves data to the most cost-effective storage class based on access patterns, making it ideal for data with unpredictable access. Other classes like Glacier Flexible Retrieval and Standard-Infrequent Access are designed for more predictable access patterns or archiving.

text: What is the AWS database service that allows you to upload data structured in key-value format?
explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed, serverless, key-value NoSQL database that makes it easy to create high-performance applications that scale globally. It is designed for fast and predictable performance with seamless scalability.

text: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding Amazon EC2 On-demand instances?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: On-Demand Instances do not require a start-up fee when launching a new instance for the first time. They follow a pay-as-you-go model with no long-term commitments or upfront payments, and Linux instances are charged per second.

answers.text: You have to pay a start-up fee when launching a new instance for the first time.
The on-demand instances follow the AWS pay-as-you-go pricing model.
With on-demand instances, no longer-term commitments or upfront payments are needed.
When using on-demand Linux instances, you are charged per second based on an hourly rate.

text: A company has moved to AWS recently. Which of the following AWS Services will help ensure that they have the proper security settings? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor provides recommendations for security, cost optimization, performance, and fault tolerance. Amazon Inspector is an automated security assessment service that helps improve the security and compliance of applications deployed on AWS.

text: What is the AWS feature that provides an additional level of security above the default authentication mechanism of usernames and passwords?
explanation: AWS Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of security to your AWS account access by requiring users to present more than one form of verification, beyond just a password.

text: A company is introducing a new product to their customers, and is expecting a surge in traffic to their web application. As part of their Enterprise Support plan, which of the following provides the company with architectural and scaling guidance?
explanation: Infrastructure Event Management (IEM) is a proactive service offered as part of AWS Enterprise Support that provides architectural and scaling guidance for significant events like product launches with expected traffic surges.

text: You work as an on-premises MySQL DBA. The work of database configuration, backups, patching, and DR can be time-consuming and repetitive. Your company has decided to migrate to the AWS Cloud. Which of the following can help save time on database maintenance so you can focus on data architecture and performance?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Amazon RDS automates and simplifies common database administration tasks such as patching, backups, and disaster recovery, allowing DBAs to focus on data architecture and performance.

text: Which of the below is a best-practice when designing solutions on AWS?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Automating infrastructure and deployments allows for easier experimentation and adaptation of architectural designs to meet evolving requirements.

answers.text: Invest heavily in architecting your environment, as it is not easy to change your design later.
Use AWS reservations to reduce costs when testing your production environment.
Automate wherever possible to make architectural (© ) experimentation easier.
Provision a large compute capacity to handle any spikes in load.

text: According to the AWS Acceptable Use Policy, which of the following statements is true regarding penetration testing of EC2 instances?
explanation: AWS allows customers to perform penetration testing on their own EC2 instances without prior authorization, as long as it is conducted responsibly and does not violate the AWS Acceptable Use Policy.

answers.text: Penetration testing is not allowed in AWS.
Penetration testing is performed automatically by AWS to determine vulnerabilities in your AWS infrastructure.
Penetration testing can be performed by the customer on their own instances without prior authorization from AWS.
The AWS customers are only allowed to perform penetration testing on services managed by AWS.

text: Which service is used to ensure that messages between software components are not lost if one or more components fail?
explanation: Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a fully managed message queuing service that enables you to decouple and scale microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. SQS standard queues offer the highest throughput, best- கிடைக்கும்தன்மை, and built-in fault tolerance for reliable message delivery.

text: The principle 'design for failure and nothing will fail' is very important when designing your AWS Cloud architecture. Which of the following would help adhere to this principle? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Availability Zones provide fault isolation within an AWS Region, and Elastic Load Balancing distributes traffic across multiple instances and Availability Zones, both contributing to high availability and resilience.

text: What is the AWS service that provides a virtual network dedicated to your AWS account?
explanation: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) allows you to provision a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network that you define. This provides a secure and private network environment for your AWS resources.

text: According to the AWS Shared responsibility model, which of the following are the responsibility of the customer? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The customer is responsible for managing and patching applications installed on Amazon EC2, and for protecting the confidentiality of data in transit, such as data being uploaded to Amazon S3.

answers.text: Managing environmental events of AWS data centers.
Protecting the confidentiality of data in transit in Amazon S3.
Controlling physical access to AWS Regions.
Ensuring that the underlying EC2 host is configured properly.
Patching applications installed on Amazon EC2.

text: Which of the following AWS services can be used as a compute resource? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers in the cloud, and AWS Lambda allows you to run code without provisioning or managing servers, both serving as compute resources.

text: Your company is designing a new application that will store and retrieve photos and videos. Which of the following services should you recommend as the underlying storage mechanism?
explanation: Amazon S3 is a highly scalable, durable, and available object storage service designed for storing and retrieving any amount of data, making it ideal for photos and videos.

text: Which of the following is equivalent to a user name and password and is used to authenticate your programmatic access to AWS services and APIs?
explanation: Access Keys, consisting of an Access Key ID and a Secret Access Key, are used to authenticate programmatic access to AWS services and APIs, similar to a username and password.

text: What does Amazon ElastiCache provide?
explanation: Amazon ElastiCache is a fully managed in-memory caching service that can be used to improve the performance of read-heavy applications. It supports Memcached and Redis.

answers.text: An Ehcache compatible in-memory data store.
An online software store that allows Customers to launch pre-configured software with just few clicks.
A domain name system in the cloud.

text: What is the AWS service that enables you to manage all of your AWS accounts from a single master account?
explanation: AWS Organizations allows you to consolidate multiple AWS accounts into an organization that you create and manage. You can then easily manage policies across your accounts, such as access control and cost management.

text: Which of the following EC2 instance purchasing options supports the Bring Your Own License (BYOL) model for almost every BYOL scenario?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Dedicated Hosts allow you to bring your own licenses (BYOL) for software that is licensed per-processor or per-core. This is because Dedicated Hosts give you visibility into the individual host hardware and its details.

text: Which of the following is one of the benefits of moving infrastructure from an on-premises data center to AWS?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Migrating to AWS shifts spending from Capital Expenditure (CapEx), which involves upfront investments in hardware and data centers, to Operational Expenditure (OpEx), which involves paying for IT resources as they are used. This allows organizations to avoid large upfront costs and pay only for what they consume.

answers.text: Free support for all enterprise customers.
AWS holds responsibility for managing customer applications.

text: Which of the following are important design principles you should adopt when designing systems on AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The AWS Well-Architected Framework outlines key design principles for building secure, high-performing, resilient, and cost-effective systems on the AWS Cloud. Automating processes and removing single points of failure are crucial for achieving these goals.

answers.text: Always use Global Services in your architecture rather than Regional Services.
Always choose to pay as you go.

text: Which AWS Service can be used to establish a dedicated, private network connection between AWS and your datacenter?
explanation: AWS Direct Connect establishes a dedicated network connection from your premises to AWS, bypassing the public internet for a more consistent network experience. Amazon CloudFront is a content delivery network, AWS Snowball is for data transfer, and Amazon Route 53 is a DNS service.

text: You are working on two projects that require completely different network configurations. Which AWS service or feature will allow you to isolate resources and network configurations?
explanation: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) allows you to provision a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network that you define. This means you can create your own IP address space, subnets, route tables, and network gateways, effectively isolating your network configurations for different projects.

text: Which of the following services can help protect your web applications from SQL injection and other vulnerabilities in your application code?
explanation: AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) is a web application firewall that helps protect your web applications or APIs against common web exploits that could affect application availability, compromise security, or consume excessive resources. It allows you to set up rules to allow or block requests based on conditions like IP addresses, HTTP headers, HTTP body, or URI strings, effectively mitigating threats such as SQL injection.

text: An organization needs to analyze and process a large number of data sets. Which AWS service should they use?
explanation: Amazon EMR (Elastic MapReduce) is a managed cluster platform that simplifies running big data frameworks, such as Apache Hadoop, Apache Spark, and Presto, on AWS for large-scale data processing and analysis.

text: Based on the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, which of the following are the sole responsibility of AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security of the cloud, which includes the hardware, software, networking, and facilities that run AWS services. Creating hypervisors and hardware maintenance fall under AWS's responsibility.

text: What is the AWS service that provides you the highest level of control over the underlying virtual infrastructure?
explanation: Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud) provides virtual servers in the cloud, giving users the highest level of control over their virtual infrastructure compared to managed database services.

text: What are the default security credentials that are required to access the AWS management console for an IAM user account?
explanation: IAM users require a username and password to log in to the AWS Management Console. MFA and access keys are additional security measures or are used for programmatic access, not default console login.

text: In your on-premises environment, you can create as many virtual servers as you need from a single template. What can you use to perform the same in AWS?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: An Amazon Machine Image (AMI) is a template that contains the software configuration (operating system, application server, and applications) required to launch an instance. You can launch multiple instances from a single AMI, similar to creating virtual servers from a template in an on-premises environment.

text: What are two advantages of using Cloud Computing over using traditional data centers? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Cloud computing offers advantages such as eliminating single points of failure through its distributed infrastructure and inherent redundancy. It also provides a distributed infrastructure that is more resilient than traditional, centralized data centers.

text: Which of the following aspects of security are managed by AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS manages the security of the global physical infrastructure, including data centers and hardware, as part of the AWS shared responsibility model. AWS also manages hardware patching, ensuring the underlying infrastructure is secure and up-to-date.

text: Which statement best describes the operational excellence pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
explanation: The operational excellence pillar focuses on running and monitoring systems to deliver business value, and continually improving supporting processes and procedures, including how to run and operate effectively.

answers.text: The ability of a system to recover gracefully from failure.
The efficient use of computing resources to meet requirements.
The ability to monitor systems and improve supporting processes and procedures.
The ability to manage datacenter operations more efficiently.

text: AWS has created a large number of Edge Locations as part of its Global Infrastructure. Which of the following is NOT a benefit of using Edge Locations?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Edge locations are primarily used by CloudFront to cache content closer to users, thereby reducing latency for content delivery. They are not designed for distributing traffic across multiple instances to reduce latency.

answers.text: Edge locations are used by CloudFront to cache the most recent responses.
Edge locations are used by CloudFront to improve your end users' experience when uploading files.
Edge locations are used by CloudFront to distribute traffic across multiple instances to reduce latency.
Edge locations are used by CloudFront to distribute content to global users with low latency.

text: What are the change management tools that helps AWS customers audit and monitor all resource changes in their AWS environment? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS CloudTrail provides a record of actions taken by a user, role, or an AWS service in AWS, while AWS Config enables you to assess, audit, and evaluate the configurations of your AWS resources.

text: Which of the following services allows you to run containerized applications on a cluster of EC2 instances?
explanation: Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) is a highly scalable, high-performance container orchestration service that supports Docker containers and allows you to run containerized applications on a cluster of EC2 instances.

text: Which of the following services will help businesses ensure compliance in AWS?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail enables governance, compliance, and risk auditing of your AWS account by logging and continuously monitoring account activity and API calls. CloudFront is a CDN, CloudWatch is for monitoring, and CloudEndure Migration is for disaster recovery.

text: Which of the following procedures will help reduce your Amazon S3 costs?
explanation: Utilizing the appropriate Amazon S3 storage classes, such as transitioning less frequently accessed data to S3 Glacier, is a key strategy for cost optimization. Different storage classes offer varying cost and retrieval performance characteristics.

answers.text: Use the Import/Export feature to move old files automatically to Amazon Glacier.
Use the right combination of storage classes based on different use cases.
Pick the right Availability Zone for your S3 bucket.
Move all the data stored in S3 standard to EBS.

text: What are the AWS services/features that can help you maintain a highly available and fault-tolerant architecture in AWS? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the number of EC2 instances to match demand, and Elastic Load Balancing distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets, both contributing to high availability and fault tolerance.

text: Which of the following activities may help reduce your AWS monthly costs?
explanation: Enabling Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling allows you to automatically adjust the number of EC2 instances based on demand, ensuring you only pay for the compute capacity you need. Over-provisioning can lead to unnecessary costs.

answers.text: Enabling Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling for all of your workloads.
Using the AWS Network Load Balancer (NLB) to load balance the incoming HTTP requests.
Removing all of your Cost Allocation Tags.
Deploying your AWS resources across multiple Availability Zones.

text: What is the AWS service/feature that takes advantage of Amazon CloudFront's globally distributed edge locations to transfer files to S3 with higher upload speeds?
explanation: S3 Transfer Acceleration uses Amazon CloudFront's globally distributed edge locations to accelerate uploads to S3. AWS WAF is a web application firewall, and AWS Snowball/Snowmobile are for large-scale data transfer, not network-based acceleration.

text: Which of the following AWS security features is associated with an EC2 instance and functions to filter incoming traffic requests?
explanation: Security Groups act as a virtual firewall for EC2 instances to control incoming and outgoing traffic. Network ACLs operate at the subnet level, VPC Flow logs record network traffic, and AWS X-Ray is for distributed tracing.

text: Which AWS services can be used to improve the performance of a global application and reduce latency for its users? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Global Accelerator improves performance by directing user traffic to the nearest AWS edge location and then routing it over the AWS global network. Amazon CloudFront is a content delivery network (CDN) that caches content closer to users, reducing latency.

text: Using Amazon RDS falls under the shared responsibility model. Which of the following are customer responsibilities? (Choose TWO)
explanation: In Amazon RDS, customers are responsible for managing the database schema and configuring database settings to meet their specific needs.

text: A company has a large amount of structured data stored in their on-premises data center. They are planning to migrate all the data to AWS, what is the most appropriate AWS database option?
explanation: Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service that supports various database engines, making it suitable for structured data migration. Amazon DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, which is typically used for unstructured or semi-structured data with different access patterns.

text: A company has created a solution that helps AWS customers improve their architectures on AWS. Which AWS program may support this company?
explanation: APN Technology Partners provide specialized technology solutions that help customers address specific technical needs for workloads running on AWS. They offer products that integrate with AWS services.

text: What is the AWS serverless service that allows you to run your applications without any administrative burden?
explanation: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that automatically runs your code in response to events, managing all the underlying infrastructure. Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS require instance management and configuration, while Amazon Lightsail offers a simplified VPS experience but is not fully serverless.

text: Jessica is managing an e-commerce web application in AWS. The application is hosted on six EC2 instances. One day, three of the instances crashed; but none of her customers were affected. What has Jessica done correctly in this scenario?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: A fault-tolerant system is designed to continue operating even if some of its components fail. By having multiple EC2 instances, Jessica ensured that the failure of three instances did not impact her customers.

answers.text: She has properly built an elastic system.
She has properly built a fault tolerant system.
She has properly built an encrypted system.
She has properly built a scalable system.

text: Where can you store files in AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) and Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) are both AWS services designed for storing files and data. EFS provides scalable file storage for EC2 instances, while EBS offers block-level storage volumes for use with EC2 instances.

text: Which AWS service can be used to store and reliably deliver messages across distributed systems?
explanation: Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a fully managed message queuing service that enables you to decouple and scale microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. It reliably stores messages and delivers them to consumers.

text: Which of the following describes the payment model that AWS makes available for customers that can commit to using Amazon EC2 over a one or 3-year term to reduce their total computing costs?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances or Savings Plans allow customers to commit to a usage term in exchange for a significant discount on compute costs compared to On-Demand pricing.

text: A company is migrating its on-premises database to Amazon RDS. What should the company do to ensure Amazon RDS costs are kept to a minimum?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Right-sizing instances before and after migration ensures that you are only paying for the resources you need, which is the most effective way to minimize costs for Amazon RDS.

answers.text: Combine On-demand Capacity Reservations with Saving Plans.

text: What is the primary storage service used by Amazon RDS database instances?
explanation: Amazon RDS database instances use Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes for their primary storage. EBS provides persistent block storage volumes for use with Amazon EC2 instances, including those managed by RDS.

text: A company is developing a new application using a microservices framework. The new application is having performance and latency issues. Which AWS Service should be used to troubleshoot these issues?
explanation: AWS X-Ray is a service that helps developers analyze and debug distributed applications, such as those built using microservices. It can trace requests as they travel through your application, helping you identify performance bottlenecks and latency issues.

text: Which of the following AWS services is designed with native Multi-AZ fault tolerance in mind? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon S3 and Amazon DynamoDB are designed with native Multi-AZ fault tolerance because they automatically store data across multiple Availability Zones within a region, ensuring high availability and durability.

text: What are the Amazon RDS features that can be used to improve the availability of your database? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Multi-AZ deployments increase availability by replicating data to a standby instance in a different Availability Zone, and Read Replicas improve availability by offloading read traffic and allowing failover.

text: Sarah has deployed an application in the Northern California (us-west-1) region. After examining the application's traffic, she notices that about 30% of the traffic is coming from Asia. What can she do to reduce latency for the users in Asia?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a global content delivery network (CDN). By caching content in edge locations closer to users in Asia, CloudFront can significantly reduce latency for those users.

answers.text: Replicate the current resources across multiple Availability Zones within the same region.
Migrate the application to a hosting provider in Asia.
Create a CDN using CloudFront, so that content is cached at Edge Locations close to and in Asia.

text: An organization runs many systems and uses many AWS products. Which of the following services enables them to control how each developer interacts with these products?
explanation: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is used to securely control access to AWS services and resources for your users. You can use IAM to create and manage AWS users and groups, and to use permissions to allow or deny their access to AWS resources.

text: Using Amazon EC2 falls under which of the following cloud computing models?
explanation: Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers in the cloud, offering infrastructure like servers, storage, and networking, which aligns with the Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) model. SaaS delivers complete applications, and PaaS provides a platform for developing and deploying applications, neither of which EC2 directly represents on its own.

text: Which of the below is a best-practice when building applications on AWS?
explanation: Decoupling application components allows them to run independently, which improves fault tolerance, scalability, and maintainability, aligning with AWS best practices.

answers.text: Strengthen physical security by applying the principle of least privilege.
Ensure that the application runs on hardware from trusted vendors.
Use IAM policies to maintain performance.
Decouple the components of the application so that they run independently.

text: A company has deployed applications on Amazon EC2 instances. The company needs to assess application vulnerabilities and must identify infrastructure deployments that do not meet best practices. Which AWS service can the company use to meet these requirements?
explanation: Amazon Inspector is an automated security assessment service that helps improve the security and compliance of applications deployed on AWS by continuously analyzing them for vulnerabilities and deviations from best practices. AWS Trusted Advisor provides recommendations across cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. AWS Config enables you to assess, audit, and evaluate the configurations of your AWS resources. Amazon GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior.

text: Amazon Glacier is an Amazon S3 storage class that is suitable for storing [...] & [...]. (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon S3 Glacier is designed for long-term archiving of data that is infrequently accessed, making it suitable for storing active archives and long-term analytic data where retrieval times are not critical.

text: What does Amazon Elastic Beanstalk provide?
explanation: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a Platform as a Service (PaaS) offering that automates the deployment and scaling of web applications and services. It is not a compute engine for ECS, a file storage solution, or a NoSQL database service.

answers.text: A PaaS solution to automate application deployment.
A compute engine for Amazon ECS.
A scalable file storage solution for use with AWS and on-premises servers.

text: What is the AWS service that performs automated network assessments of Amazon EC2 instances to check for vulnerabilities?
explanation: Amazon Inspector is an automated security assessment service that helps improve the security and compliance of applications deployed on AWS by performing automated security scans. Security groups and Network Access Control Lists (NACLs) are firewall components, and Amazon Kinesis is a streaming data service.

text: Under the Shared Responsibility Model, which of the following controls do customers fully inherit from AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Customers fully inherit AWS's environmental and physical controls. AWS is responsible for securing the underlying infrastructure, including the physical security of data centers and the environment in which services operate.

text: A company needs to host a database in Amazon RDS for at least three years. Which of the following options would be the most cost-effective solution?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: For a long-term commitment of three years for Amazon RDS, a Reserved Instance with a partial upfront payment offers greater savings compared to no upfront or on-demand instances, and Spot Instances are not suitable for production databases due to their interruptible nature.

text: Your application has recently experienced significant global growth, and international users are complaining of high latency. What is the AWS characteristic that can help improve your international users' experience?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS's global reach, with its network of Regions and Availability Zones worldwide, allows you to deploy applications closer to your users, reducing latency and improving their experience.

text: Savings Plans are available for which of the following AWS compute services? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: AWS Compute Savings Plans offer a lower price compared to On-Demand prices, in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment to consistent usage of compute services. They apply to Amazon EC2, AWS Lambda, and AWS Fargate usage.

text: A company has business critical workloads hosted on AWS and they are unwilling to accept any downtime. Which of the following is a recommended best practice to protect their workloads in the event of an unexpected natural disaster?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Deploying AWS resources to another AWS Region and implementing an Active-Active disaster recovery strategy ensures that workloads remain available even if an entire AWS Region becomes unavailable.

answers.text: Replicate data across multiple Edge Locations worldwide and use Amazon CloudFront to perform automatic failover in the event of an outage.
Deploy AWS resources across multiple Availability Zones within the same AWS Region.
Create point-in-time backups in another subnet and recover this data when a disaster occurs.
Deploy AWS resources to another AWS Region and implement an Active-Active disaster recovery strategy.

text: Which statement is correct with regards to AWS service limits? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS service limits can be increased by contacting AWS Support, and AWS Trusted Advisor can be used to monitor them. Service limits are account-specific and differ across services, so IAM users do not necessarily have the same limits.

answers.text: You can contact AWS support to increase the service limits.
Each IAM user has the same service limit.
There are no service limits on AWS.
You can use the AWS Trusted Advisor to monitor your service limits.
The Amazon Simple Email Service is responsible for sending email notifications when usage approaches a service limit.

text: What is the AWS tool that enables you to use scripts to manage all AWS services and resources?
explanation: The AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI) is a unified tool to manage your AWS cloud services with commands that are entered expositionally into a terminal program, allowing you to control multiple AWS services through scripting.

text: What are the connectivity options that can be used to build hybrid cloud architectures? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides dedicated network connections from your premises to AWS. AWS VPN establishes secure, encrypted tunnels over the public internet between your on-premises network and your VPC.

text: A company has deployed a new web application on multiple Amazon EC2 instances. Which of the following should they use to ensure that the incoming HTTP traffic is distributed evenly across the instances?
explanation: An Application Load Balancer (ALB) distributes incoming HTTP and HTTPS traffic across multiple Amazon EC2 instances, ensuring even traffic distribution and high availability for web applications.

text: Which of the following AWS offerings is a MySQL-compatible relational database service that can scale capacity automatically based on demand?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon Aurora is a MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database that offers enhanced performance and availability, including automatic scaling of capacity.

text: Which of the following can help protect your EC2 instances from DDoS attacks? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Network ACLs and Security Groups are stateful and stateless firewalls used to control inbound and outbound traffic to EC2 instances, acting as primary defenses against DDoS attacks. AWS Shield is a managed DDoS protection service.

text: What is the AWS data warehouse service that supports a high level of query performance on large amounts of datasets?
explanation: Amazon Redshift is a fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service designed for high-performance analysis of large datasets.

text: Which of the following should be considered when performing a TCO analysis to compare the costs of running an application on AWS instead of on-premises?
explanation: When comparing Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) between on-premises and AWS, an organization should consider the direct costs of physical hardware, as this represents a significant capital expenditure on-premises that is replaced by AWS's pay-as-you-go model.

text: How are AWS customers billed for Linux-based Amazon EC2 usage?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: AWS charges for Linux-based Amazon EC2 instances based on per-second billing with a minimum charge of one minute. This means you are charged for the actual duration your instance runs, rounded up to the nearest minute.

answers.text: EC2 instances will be billed on one second increments, with a minimum of one minute.
EC2 instances will be billed on one hour increments, with a minimum of one day.
EC2 instances will be billed on one minute increments, with a minimum of one hour.
EC2 instances will be billed on one day increments, with a minimum of one month.

text: Which of the following will impact the price paid for an EC2 instance? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: The instance type directly affects the price due to differences in CPU, memory, and other resources, and pricing can vary slightly between Availability Zones within the same Region.

answers.text: The Availability Zone where the instance is provisioned.

text: A customer spent a lot of time configuring a newly deployed Amazon EC2 instance. After the workload increases, the customer decides to provision another EC2 instance with an identical configuration. How can the customer achieve this?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: An Amazon Machine Image (AMI) is a template that contains the software configuration (operating system, application server, and applications) required to launch an EC2 instance. Creating an AMI from an existing instance captures its configuration, allowing you to launch identical instances.

answers.text: By creating an AWS Config template from the old instance and launching a new instance from it.
By creating an EBS Snapshot of the old instance.
By installing Aurora on EC2 and launching a new instance from it.
By creating an AMI from the old instance and launching a new instance from it.

text: A company uses AWS Organizations to manage all of its AWS accounts. Which of the following allows the company to restrict what services and actions are allowed in each individual account?
explanation: AWS Service Control Policies (SCPs) are a feature of AWS Organizations that you can use to define a maximum permissions level, or boundary, for all IAM principals in an AWS account. SCPs do not grant permissions; they are used to set the guardrails for what actions and services can be performed in an account.

text: Which of the following statements describes the AWS Cloud's agility?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Agility in the AWS Cloud refers to the ability to provision resources quickly, allowing for rapid experimentation and deployment. Provisioning resources in minutes directly reflects this characteristic.

answers.text: AWS allows you to host your applications in multiple regions around the world.
AWS provides customizable hardware at the lowest possible cost.
AWS allows you to provision resources in minutes.
AWS allows you to pay upfront to reduce costs.

text: What are the benefits of using the Amazon Relational Database Service? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Amazon RDS reduces administrative tasks such as hardware provisioning, database setup, patching, and backups, and it allows for easy scaling of compute capacity by changing instance types.

answers.text: Complete control over the underlying host.
Scales automatically to larger or smaller instance types.
Supports the document and key-value data structure.

text: What is the connectivity option that uses Internet Protocol Security (IPSec) to establish encrypted connectivity between an on-premises network and the AWS Cloud?
explanation: AWS Site-to-Site VPN establishes encrypted connections between your on-premises network and your Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) using IPSec. An Internet Gateway allows communication between your VPC and the internet, and AWS IQ is a service for finding AWS experts.

text: What is the minimum level of AWS support that provides 24x7 access to technical support engineers via phone and chat?
explanation: Business Support is the minimum AWS Support plan that provides 24x7 access to technical support engineers via phone, chat, and email.

text: Which of the following is used to control network traffic in AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Network Access Control Lists (NACLs) and Security Groups are both used to control network traffic in AWS. NACLs operate at the subnet level and are stateless, while Security Groups operate at the instance level and are stateful.

text: A company has developed a media transcoding application in AWS. The application is designed to recover quickly from hardware failures. Which one of the following types of instance would be the most cost-effective choice to use?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Spot Instances offer significant cost savings compared to On-Demand or Reserved Instances. They are ideal for fault-tolerant applications like media transcoding that can quickly recover from interruptions.

text: Which AWS Service provides the current status of all AWS Services in all AWS Regions?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: The AWS Service Health Dashboard displays the status of all AWS services in all AWS Regions, providing a global view of service health.

text: Which AWS service or feature can be used to call AWS Services from different programming languages?
explanation: The AWS Software Development Kits (SDKs) provide libraries and APIs that allow developers to make programmatic calls to AWS services from various programming languages.

text: Which AWS Service can be used to register a new domain name?
explanation: Amazon Route 53 is a highly available and scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service that enables you to register domain names and manage DNS records. AWS KMS is for key management, AWS Config is for configuration management, and Amazon Personalize is for machine learning personalization.

text: App development companies move their business to AWS to reduce time-to-market and improve customer satisfaction, what are the AWS automation tools that help them deploy their applications faster? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS CloudFormation and AWS Elastic Beanstalk are both automation tools that help deploy applications faster. CloudFormation uses infrastructure as code for provisioning, and Elastic Beanstalk automates deployment and management.

text: Which AWS service provides cost-optimization recommendations?
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor inspects your AWS environment and provides recommendations across cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. AWS Pricing Calculator is used to estimate costs, and Amazon QuickSight is for business intelligence.

text: A company has hundreds of VPCs in multiple AWS Regions worldwide. What service does AWS offer to simplify the connection management among the VPCs?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Transit Gateway acts as a central hub that simplifies network connectivity by allowing you to connect thousands of VPCs and on-premises networks. VPC peering connects only two VPCs at a time, and security groups are for instance-level firewalls, not inter-VPC connectivity management.

text: What is one benefit and one drawback of buying a reserved EC2 instance? (Select TWO)
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Reserved instances offer a significant discount compared to on-demand instances, but they require a commitment of at least one year. They are not suitable for periodic workloads as the commitment is long-term.

answers.text: Instances can be shut down by AWS at any time with no notification.
Reserved instances require at least a one-year pricing commitment.
There is no additional charge for using dedicated instances.
Reserved instances provide a significant discount compared to on-demand instances.
Reserved instances are best suited for periodic workloads.

text: Why does every AWS Region contain multiple Availability Zones?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Multiple Availability Zones within an AWS Region are designed to be highly resilient and isolated from failures in other Availability Zones. Deploying resources across multiple AZs allows for high availability and fault tolerance.

answers.text: Multiple Availability Zones allows you to build resilient and highly available architectures.
Multiple Availability Zones results in lower total cost compared to deploying in a single Availability Zone.
Multiple Availability Zones allows for data replication and global reach.
Multiple Availability Zones within a region increases the storage capacity available in that region.

text: What is the most cost-effective purchasing option for running a set of EC2 instances that must always be available for a period of two months?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: For a fixed two-month period where instances must always be available, Reserved Instances provide significant cost savings compared to On-Demand instances. While Spot Instances are cheaper, they can be interrupted, making them unsuitable for Always-On requirements.

text: Which of the following is a benefit of running an application in multiple Availability Zones?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Running an application in multiple Availability Zones (AZs) increases its availability by providing redundancy. If one AZ becomes unavailable, the application can continue to run in another AZ.

answers.text: Allows you to exceed AWS service limits.
Reduces application response time between servers and global users.
Increases the availability of your application.

text: Data security is one of the top priorities of AWS. How does AWS deal with old storage devices that have reached the end of their useful life?
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security *of* the cloud, including the physical security of their data centers and the secure disposal of hardware. They ensure that storage devices are destroyed in accordance with industry-standard practices to protect customer data.

answers.text: AWS sells the old devices to other hosting providers.
AWS destroys the old devices in accordance with industry-standard practices.
AWS sends the old devices for remanufacturing.
AWS stores the old devices in a secure place.

text: A developer needs to set up an SSL security certificate for a client's eCommerce website in order to use the HTTPS protocol. Which of the following AWS services can be used to deploy the required SSL server certificates? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) is used to provision, manage, and deploy public and private SSL/TLS certificates for use with AWS services. While IAM is not directly used for certificate deployment, it's essential for managing permissions related to accessing and using these certificates within AWS.

text: Which of the following AWS services scale automatically without your intervention? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Lambda automatically scales based on the number of incoming requests. Amazon S3 also scales automatically to handle varying workloads, providing high availability and durability for your data.

text: A company is planning to migrate an application from Amazon EC2 to AWS Lambda to use a serverless architecture. Which of the following will be the responsibility of AWS after migration? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: When migrating to AWS Lambda for a serverless architecture, AWS takes responsibility for operating system maintenance and capacity management, as these are handled by the managed service.

text: How do ELBs improve the reliability of your application?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) improves application reliability by distributing incoming traffic across multiple healthy targets, such as EC2 instances. This ensures that traffic is only sent to instances that are able to respond, preventing requests from being sent to failing targets.

answers.text: By distributing traffic across multiple S3 buckets.
By replicating data to multiple availability zones.
By ensuring that only healthy targets receive traffic.

text: A company needs to migrate their website from on-premises to AWS. Security is a major concern for them, so they need to host their website on hardware that is NOT shared with other AWS customers. Which of the following EC2 instance options meets this requirement?
explanation: Dedicated instances provide hardware that is not shared with other AWS customers, fulfilling the security requirement. On-demand, Spot, and Reserved instances may share underlying hardware with other customers.

text: A customer is planning to move billions of images and videos to be stored on Amazon S3. The customer has approximately 60 Petabytes of data to move. Which of the following AWS Services is the best choice to transfer the data to AWS?
explanation: AWS Snowmobile is designed for large-scale data transfers, specifically when dealing with hundreds of petabytes of data to be moved to AWS. For 60 petabytes, Snowmobile is the most suitable and efficient service.

text: A company plans to migrate a large amount of archived data to AWS. The archived data must be maintained for a period of 5 years and must be retrievable within 5 hours of a request. What is the most cost-effective AWS storage service to use?
explanation: Amazon S3 Glacier is the most cost-effective storage service for archiving data that is infrequently accessed but must be available within a few hours. It offers lower storage costs than Amazon S3 Standard, Amazon EFS, or Amazon EBS, while still meeting the retrieval time requirement.

text: Which AWS Service is used to manage user permissions?
explanation: AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management) is the service used to manage user permissions and access to AWS resources. Security Groups manage network traffic, Amazon ECS manages containers, and AWS Support provides technical assistance.

text: Which support plan includes AWS Support Concierge Service?
explanation: The Enterprise Support plan is the highest tier of AWS Support and includes access to the AWS Support Concierge Service.

text: A company needs to track resource changes using the API call history. Which AWS service can help the company achieve this goal?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail provides a history of AWS API calls for your AWS account, including calls made through the AWS Management Console, AWS SDKs, and command-line tools. This allows you to track resource changes by reviewing the API call history.

text: What are the benefits of using an AWS-managed service? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS-managed services reduce operational complexity by handling tasks like patching and infrastructure management, and they accelerate solution delivery by abstracting away undifferentiated heavy lifting.

answers.text: Provides complete control over the virtual infrastructure.
Allows customers to deliver new solutions faster.
Eliminates the need to encrypt data.
Allows developers to control all patching related activities.

text: Which of the following are use cases for Amazon S3? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon S3 is suitable for hosting static websites and can serve as a media store for Amazon CloudFront, a content delivery network service.

answers.text: Hosting websites that require sustained high CPU utilization.
A media store for the CloudFront service.
Processing data streams at any scale.

text: What is the AWS' recommendation regarding access keys?
explanation: AWS recommends rotating access keys regularly to enhance security by limiting the window of exposure if a key is compromised.
