answers.text: Delete all access keys and use passwords instead.
Only share them with trusted people.
Save them within your application code.

text: What is the AWS IAM feature that provides an additional layer of security on top of user-name and password authentication?
explanation: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is an AWS IAM feature that provides an extra layer of security by requiring more than one authentication method beyond just a username and password.

text: What is the benefit of using an API to access AWS Services?
explanation: AWS APIs allow developers to interact with AWS services programmatically, enabling automation and integration of AWS resources into applications and workflows.

answers.text: It improves the performance of AWS resources.
It reduces the time needed to provision AWS resources.
It reduces the number of developers necessary.
It allows for programmatic management of AWS resources.

text: A company is planning to migrate a database with high read/write activity to AWS. What is the best storage option to use?
explanation: Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) provides persistent block storage volumes for use with Amazon EC2 instances, making it suitable for database workloads requiring high read/write activity.

text: How can AWS customers track and avoid over-spending on underutilized reserved instances?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: AWS Budgets allows customers to track their AWS costs and usage, and to set alerts for when costs or usage exceed or are forecasted to exceed a budget. This enables them to proactively manage spending on reserved instances.

answers.text: Customers can add all AWS accounts to an AWS Organization, enable Consolidated Billing, and turn off Reserved Instance sharing.
Customers can use Amazon Neptune to track and analyze their usage patterns, detect underutilized reserved instances, and then sell them on the Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance Marketplace.
Customers can use the AWS Budgets service to track the reserved instances usage and set up alert notifications when their utilization drops below the threshold that they define.
Customers can use Amazon CloudTrail to automatically check for unused reservations and get recommendations to reduce their bill.

text: What is the AWS service that provides five times the performance of a standard MySQL database?
explanation: Amazon Aurora is designed to be highly performant and compatible with MySQL, offering up to five times the throughput of standard MySQL.

text: What does AWS Service Catalog provide?
explanation: AWS Service Catalog allows IT administrators to create and manage catalogs of IT services that are approved for use on AWS. This helps in organizing and governing commonly deployed IT services for end-users.

answers.text: It enables customers to quickly find descriptions and use cases for AWS services.
It enables customers to explore the different catalogs of AWS services.
It simplifies organizing and governing commonly deployed IT services.
It allows developers to deploy infrastructure on AWS using familiar programming languages.

text: For managed services like Amazon DynamoDB, which of the below is AWS responsible for? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS is responsible for the underlying infrastructure and operational tasks for managed services. This includes patching the database software and performing operating system maintenance to ensure the service is secure and available.

text: Which of the following AWS Services helps with planning application migration to the AWS Cloud?
explanation: AWS Application Discovery Service helps you plan your cloud migration by collecting information about your on-premises applications, servers, and their dependencies.

text: A company is trying to analyze the costs applied to their AWS account recently. Which of the following provides them the most granular data about their AWS costs and usage?
explanation: AWS Cost & Usage Report (CUR) provides the most granular data by delivering comprehensive cost and usage data at a resource level. AWS Cost Explorer offers visualization and analysis but aggregates data, and Amazon CloudWatch monitors performance and operational metrics, not detailed cost breakdowns.

text: Which statement best describes the concept of an AWS region?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: An AWS Region is a physical location around the world where AWS has data centers. Each AWS Region consists of multiple, isolated, and available Availability Zones.

answers.text: An AWS Region is a geographical location with a collection of Edge locations.
An AWS Region is a virtual network dedicated only to a single AWS customer.
An AWS Region is a geographical location with a collection of Availability Zones.
An AWS Region represents the country where the AWS infrastructure exist.

text: A company has discovered that multiple S3 buckets were deleted, but it is unclear who deleted the buckets. Which of the following can the company use to determine the identity that deleted the buckets?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS CloudTrail records API calls made in your AWS account, including calls that delete S3 buckets. By examining CloudTrail logs, you can identify the user or role that performed the deletion.

text: Which of the following are factors in determining the appropriate database technology to use for a specific workload? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The number of reads and writes per second (throughput) and the nature of the queries (e.g., complex analytical queries vs. simple key-value lookups) are critical factors in selecting a database service that can meet performance and functional requirements.

answers.text: The number of reads and writes per second.

text: What are the benefits of implementing a tagging strategy for AWS resources? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Tagging resources allows for easy identification of resources associated with specific projects and helps in tracking AWS spending across groups of resources.

answers.text: Quickly identify resources that belong to a specific project.
Quickly identify software solutions on AWS.
Track API calls in your AWS account.
Quickly identify deleted resources and their metadata.
Track AWS spending across multiple resources.

text: What are AWS shared controls?
explanation: Shared controls are security controls that apply to both AWS's responsibility for the infrastructure and the customer's responsibility for what they deploy on that infrastructure.

answers.text: Controls that are solely the responsibility of the customer based on the application they are deploying within AWS services.
Controls that a customer inherits from AWS.
Controls that apply to both the infrastructure layer and customer layers.
Controls that the customer and AWS collaborate together upon to secure the infrastructure.

text: Which design principles relate to performance efficiency in AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Performance efficiency in the AWS Well-Architected Framework involves designing systems that can meet user demand and utilize resources efficiently. Building multi-region architectures serves global customers better, and using serverless architectures can improve scalability and responsiveness, both contributing to performance efficiency.

answers.text: Build multi-region architectures to better serve global customers.
Implement strong Identity and Access controls.

text: Which of the below are responsibilities of the customer when using Amazon EC2? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Customers are responsible for protecting their data and installing/configuring third-party software when using Amazon EC2, as part of the shared responsibility model.

answers.text: Setup and operation of managed databases.

text: Why would an organization decide to use AWS over an on-premises data center? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS offers elastic resources that allow organizations to scale their IT infrastructure up or down as needed, and it can lead to significant cost savings compared to the capital expenditures and ongoing operational costs of an on-premises data center.

text: Which of the following AWS services can help you perform security analysis and regulatory compliance auditing? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon Inspector helps automate security vulnerability assessments, and AWS Config enables continuous compliance monitoring of your AWS resources.

text: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2)?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers and is not a serverless service; serverless services abstract away server management. EC2 offers scalability and eliminates upfront hardware investment.

answers.text: Amazon EC2 is considered a Serverless Web Service.
Amazon EC2 eliminates the need to invest in hardware upfront.
Amazon EC2 can launch as many or as few virtual servers as needed.

text: What is the AWS Compute service that executes code only when triggered by events?
explanation: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that runs code in response to events and automatically manages the underlying compute resources. Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers (instances) in the cloud, which require manual provisioning and management.

text: Both AWS and traditional IT distributors provide a wide range of virtual servers to meet their customers' requirements. What is the name of these virtual servers in AWS?
explanation: Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) instances are virtual servers in AWS that provide resizable compute capacity for a wide range of workloads. They are the AWS equivalent of virtual servers offered by traditional IT distributors.

text: What is the framework created by AWS Professional Services that helps organizations design a road map to successful cloud adoption?
explanation: The AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) is a set of best practices and guidance created by AWS Professional Services to help organizations successfully adopt the AWS Cloud.

text: TYMO Cloud Corp is looking forward to migrating their entire on-premises data center to AWS. What tool can they use to perform a cost-benefit analysis of moving to the AWS Cloud?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: The AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator helps estimate the cost savings you can achieve by running your applications on AWS compared to on-premises infrastructure. It allows for a cost-benefit analysis of cloud migration.

text: Which of the following activities supports the Operational Excellence pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
explanation: Using AWS CloudFormation to manage infrastructure as code aligns with the Operational Excellence pillar by enabling repeatable, reliable, and automated deployments, reducing manual errors and improving consistency.

answers.text: Using AWS Trusted Advisor to find underutilized resources.
Using AWS CloudTrail to record user activities.
Using AWS CloudFormation to manage infrastructure as code.
Deploying an application in multiple Availability Zones.

text: Why do many startup companies prefer AWS over traditional on-premises solutions? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Startups can reduce time-to-market by leveraging AWS to focus on their core business instead of managing infrastructure, and they can replace large upfront capital expenditures with variable operational costs, making it more financially flexible.

answers.text: AWS allows them to pay later when their business succeed.
AWS can build complete data centers faster than any other Cloud provider.
Using AWS, they can reduce time-to-market by focusing on business activities rather than on building and managing data centers.
AWS removes the need to invest in operational expenditure.
Using AWS allows companies to replace large capital expenditure with low variable costs.

text: What are the benefits of using DynamoDB? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon DynamoDB automatically scales to meet throughput demands and offers extremely low, single-digit millisecond latency for predictable performance. Resizable instances and support for relational models are characteristics of relational databases like Amazon RDS, not DynamoDB, and it does not directly support CouchDB or MongoDB engines.

answers.text: Automatically scales to meet required throughput capacity.
Provides resizable instances to match the current demand.
Supports both relational and non-relational data models.
Supports the most popular NoSQL database engines such as CouchDB and MongoDB.

text: Which of the following can be used to protect data at rest on Amazon S3? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Versioning protects against accidental deletions or overwrites by keeping multiple versions of an object. Permissions, specifically access control lists (ACLs) and bucket policies, restrict who can access the data at rest in S3.

text: As part of the AWS Migration Acceleration Program (MAP), what does AWS provide to accelerate Enterprise adoption of AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS provides access to AWS Partners and AWS Professional Services as part of the Migration Acceleration Program (MAP) to help enterprises accelerate their adoption of AWS.

text: AWS recommends some practices to help organizations avoid unexpected charges on their bill. Which of the following is NOT one of these practices?
explanation: Deleting unused Auto Scaling launch configurations does not incur charges. Practices like deleting unattached EBS volumes, unassigned Elastic IPs, and idle Elastic Load Balancers help reduce costs.

answers.text: Deleting unused EBS volumes after terminating an EC2instance.
Releasing unused Elastic IPs after terminating an EC2instance.

text: What is the AWS tool that can help a company visualize their AWS spending in the last few months?
explanation: AWS Cost Explorer is the correct tool as it allows users to visualize, understand, and manage their AWS costs and usage over time. It provides interactive charts and tables to analyze spending patterns.

text: When running a workload in AWS, the customer is NOT responsible for: (Select TWO)
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security *of* the cloud, including data center operations and the underlying infrastructure. Customers are responsible for security *in* the cloud, which includes managing penetration tests, reserving capacity, and auditing their own regulatory compliance.

text: Which AWS service can be used to send promotional text messages (SMS) to more than 200 countries worldwide?
explanation: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) is a managed service that enables you to decouple and scale microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. It supports sending messages to various endpoints, including SMS, for global reach.

text: Which of the following allows you to create new RDS instances? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS CloudFormation allows you to model and provision your AWS infrastructure, including RDS instances, using code. The AWS Management Console provides a graphical interface to create and manage RDS instances interactively.

text: One of the major advantages of using AWS is cost savings. What does AWS provide to reduce the cost of running Amazon EC2 instances?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: AWS offers per-second billing for Amazon EC2 instances, allowing customers to pay only for the compute time they consume. This model helps reduce costs by avoiding charges for unused periods.

text: Which AWS Group assists customers in achieving their desired business outcomes?
explanation: AWS Professional Services partners with customers to help them achieve their desired business outcomes by providing expert guidance and support. AWS Trusted Advisor offers recommendations for cost savings, performance, security, fault tolerance, and other best practices. The AWS Security Team focuses on security for AWS itself, and there is no specific AWS Concierge Support Team offering, though AWS Support has different plans and offerings.

text: Which AWS service or feature is used to manage the keys used to encrypt customer data?
explanation: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) is a managed service that simplifies the creation and control of encryption keys used to encrypt your data. SCPs are for access control, MFA is for authentication, and Macie is for data security and risk identification.

text: Which AWS Service allows customers to download AWS SOC & PCI reports?
explanation: AWS Artifact is a service that provides on-demand download of AWS compliance reports, such as SOC and PCI, to help customers demonstrate security and compliance to auditors.

text: A company is using EC2 Instances to run their e-commerce site on the AWS platform. If the site becomes unavailable, the company will lose a significant amount of money for each minute the site is unavailable. Which design principle should the company use to minimize the risk of an outage?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Fault tolerance is the ability of a system to continue operating even if some of its components fail. Designing for fault tolerance on AWS minimizes the risk of an outage and ensures continuous availability for critical applications.

text: You decide to buy a reserved instance for a term of one year. Which option provides the largest total discount?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Purchasing a Reserved Instance with an all-up-front payment option provides the largest total discount compared to partial or no upfront payments for the same term.

answers.text: All reserved instance payment options provide the same discount level.

text: What features does AWS offer to help protect your data in the Cloud? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS provides access control mechanisms like IAM to manage who can access your data and data encryption services to protect data at rest and in transit.

text: An AWS customer has used one Amazon Linux instance for 2 hours, 5 minutes and 9 seconds, and one CentOS instance for 4 hours, 23 minutes and 7 seconds. How much time will the customer be billed for?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: AWS bills EC2 instances by the second, with a one-minute minimum charge. Therefore, the customer will be billed for the exact duration each instance was running.

answers.text: 3 hours for the Linux instance and 5 hours for the CentOS instance.
2 hours, 5 minutes and 9 seconds for the Linux instance and 4 hours, 23 minutes and 7 seconds for the CentOS instance.
2 hours, 5 minutes and 9 seconds for the Linux instance and 5 hours for the CentOS instance.
3 hours for the Linux instance and 4 hours, 23 minutes and 7 seconds for the CentOS instance.

text: What is the AWS Support feature that allows customers to manage support cases programmatically?
explanation: The AWS Support API allows customers to programmatically create, manage, and update support cases, enabling automation of support workflows.

text: Which methods can be used by customers to interact with AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Customers can interact with AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) programmatically using AWS SDKs and via the command line using the AWS CLI. Security Groups and Network ACLs are for network security, and AWS CodeCommit is for Git repository services.

text: Which of the following are types of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) identities? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS IAM entities that can be assumed by users or services are IAM Roles, and granular access is granted to individual AWS entities through IAM Users.

text: Which of the following Amazon RDS features facilitates offloading of database read activity?
explanation: Read Replicas are separate database instances that are available to offload read traffic from your primary database instance. Database Snapshots and Automated Backups are for data recovery and point-in-time restore, while Multi-AZ Deployments provide high availability by replicating data to a standby instance.

text: How does AWS notify customers about security and privacy events pertaining to AWS services?
explanation: AWS uses Security Bulletins to inform customers about security and privacy events related to AWS services. This is the primary channel for such notifications.

text: Which IAM entity can best be used to grant temporary access to your AWS resources?
explanation: IAM Roles provide temporary security credentials that can be used to grant access to specific AWS resources, making them ideal for granting temporary access.

text: A company has a web application that is hosted on a single EC2 instance and is approaching 100 percent CPU Utilization during peak loads. Rather than scaling the server vertically, the company has decided to deploy three Amazon EC2 instances in parallel and to distribute traffic across the three servers. What AWS Service should the company use to distribute the traffic evenly?
explanation: AWS Application Load Balancer (ALB) is designed to distribute incoming application traffic across multiple EC2 instances. It operates at the application layer (Layer 7) and is ideal for this scenario.

text: Which of the following approaches will help you eliminate human error and automate the process of creating and updating your AWS environment?
explanation: Using code to provision and operate your AWS infrastructure, also known as Infrastructure as Code (IaC), allows for automated, repeatable, and error-free creation and updates of your environment.

answers.text: Use AWS CodeDeploy to build and automate your AWS environment.
Use code to provision and operate your AWS infrastructure.
Migrate all of your applications to a dedicated host.

text: A company is seeking to better secure its AWS account from unauthorized access. Which of the below options can the customer use to achieve this goal?
explanation: Requiring Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all IAM User access adds an extra layer of security, making it much harder for unauthorized users to gain access even if they obtain a user's password.

answers.text: Restrict any API call made through SDKs or CLI.
Create one IAM account for each department in the company (Development, QA, Production), and share it across all staff in that department.
Require Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all IAM User access.

text: Which AWS Service offers volume discounts based on usage?
explanation: Amazon S3 offers reduced prices per GB for storage as your usage increases through volume-based discounts.

text: Which of the following factors should be considered when determining the region in which AWS Resources will be deployed? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: When deploying AWS resources, data sovereignty requirements dictate compliance with local laws, and cost is a significant factor as pricing varies by region. Geographic proximity and the number of VPCs are not primary drivers for region selection.

answers.text: Geographic proximity to the company's location.

text: You are running a financial services web application on AWS. The application uses a MySQL database to store the data. Which of the following AWS services would improve the performance of your application by allowing you to retrieve information from fast in-memory caches?
explanation: Amazon ElastiCache provides in-memory caching services (like Redis and Memcached) that can significantly improve application performance by reducing database load and speeding up data retrieval. DAX is specific to DynamoDB, and while it provides in-memory caching, it's not compatible with MySQL.

text: What are the advantages of using Auto Scaling Groups for EC2 instances?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Auto Scaling Groups deploy EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones, which increases application availability and fault tolerance by ensuring that the application remains accessible even if one Availability Zone experiences an outage.

answers.text: Auto Scaling Groups caches the most recent responses at global edge locations to reduce latency and improve performance.
Auto Scaling Groups scales EC2 instances in multiple Availability Zones to increase application availability and fault tolerance.
Auto Scaling Groups scales EC2 instances across multiple regions to reduce latency for global users.
Auto Scaling Groups distributes application traffic across multiple Availability Zones to enhance performance.

text: The TCO gap between AWS infrastructure and traditional infrastructure has widened over the recent years. Which of the following could be the reason for that?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS continually invests in optimizing its infrastructure and services, which allows it to pass cost savings onto customers, thus lowering the overall cost of cloud computing and widening the TCO gap compared to traditional infrastructure.

answers.text: AWS helps customers invest more in capital expenditures.
AWS automates all infrastructure operations, so customers save more on human resources costs.
AWS continues to lower the cost of cloud computing for its customers.
AWS secures AWS resources at no additional charge.

text: Which of the following are examples of the customer's responsibility to implement 'security IN the cloud'? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The customer is responsible for security 'in the cloud', which includes patching and encrypting data. AWS is responsible for security 'of the cloud' and the underlying infrastructure.

answers.text: Building a schema for an application.
Patch management of the underlying infrastructure.

text: Which of the following is a type of MFA device that customers can use to protect their AWS resources?
explanation: A U2F Security Key is a hardware device that supports the Universal 2nd Factor (U2F) authentication protocol, which can be used as a second factor for Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on AWS.

text: A company is seeking to deploy an existing .NET application onto AWS as quickly as possible. Which AWS Service should the customer use to achieve this goal?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a fully managed service that provides a fast and simple way to deploy and scale web applications and services. It automates the deployment, from capacity provisioning, load balancing, auto-scaling to application health monitoring.

text: Which of the following is NOT a factor when estimating the costs of Amazon EC2? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: The number of security groups and hosted zones do not directly impact Amazon EC2 compute costs. Costs are primarily determined by instance runtime, number of instances, and allocated Elastic IP Addresses.

answers.text: The amount of time the instances will be running.

text: Which AWS Service helps enterprises extend their on-premises storage to AWS in a cost-effective manner?
explanation: AWS Storage Gateway is a hybrid cloud storage service that provides on-premises applications with access to virtually unlimited cloud storage. It helps enterprises seamlessly extend their on-premises storage to AWS.

text: A company is building an online cloud storage platform. They need a storage service that can scale capacity automatically, while minimizing cost. Which AWS storage service should the company use to meet these requirements?
explanation: Amazon S3 is a highly scalable and cost-effective object storage service that automatically scales capacity. It is ideal for online cloud storage platforms that require flexibility and cost optimization.

text: You have just hired a skilled sys-admin to join your team. As usual, you have created a new IAM user for him to interact with AWS services. On his first day, you ask him to create snapshots of all existing Amazon EBS volumes and save them in a new Amazon S3 bucket. However, the new member reports back that he is unable to create neither EBS snapshots nor S3 buckets. What might prevent him from doing this simple task?
explanation: By default, new IAM users may not have explicit permissions to perform actions like creating EBS snapshots or S3 buckets. An explicit deny statement in an IAM policy would prevent these actions, even if other policies might otherwise allow them.

answers.text: EBS and S3 are accessible only to the root account owner.
The systems administrator must contact AWS Support first to activate his new IAM account.
There is not enough space in S3 to store the snapshots.
There is a non-explicit deny to all new users.

text: An external auditor is requesting a log of all accesses to the AWS resources in the company's account. Which of the following services will provide the auditor with the requested information?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail provides a history of AWS account activity, including actions taken by users, roles, or AWS services. This log of events is crucial for security analysis and operational troubleshooting, fulfilling the auditor's request.

text: Which of the below options is true of Amazon Cloud Directory?
explanation: Amazon Cloud Directory is a fully managed, highly scalable, multi-tenant directory service that provides organizational structure for directory data across multiple dimensions. It allows for complex data hierarchies to be represented and queried efficiently.

answers.text: Amazon Cloud Directory allows the organization of hierarchies of data across multiple dimensions.
Amazon Cloud Directory enables the analysis of video and data streams in real time.
Amazon Cloud Directory allows users to access AWS with their existing Active Directory credentials.
Amazon Cloud Directory allows for registration and management of domain names.

text: A user has opened a 'Production System Down' support case to get help from AWS Support after a production system disruption. What is the expected response time for this type of support case?
explanation: A 'Production System Down' case corresponds to the Business support plan, which guarantees a response time of one hour for production system down issues.

text: Which of the below options is a best practice for making your application on AWS highly available?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Deploying your application to at least two Availability Zones (AZs) provides fault tolerance. If one AZ experiences an outage, your application can continue to run in another.

answers.text: Deploy the application to at least two Availability Zones.
Use Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) across multiple AWS Regions.
Deploy the application code on at least two servers in the same Availability Zone.
Rewrite the application code to handle all incoming requests.

text: Which of the following should be taken into account when performing a TCO analysis regarding the costs of running an application on AWS VS on-premises? (Choose TWO)
explanation: When performing a TCO analysis, you should consider both direct and indirect costs. Labor and IT costs (such as staffing, maintenance, and support) and physical infrastructure costs (like cooling and power consumption) are significant factors in on-premises deployments that are absorbed by the AWS service costs.

text: Your company requires a response time of less than 15 minutes from support interactions about their business-critical systems that are hosted on AWS if those systems go down. Which AWS Support Plan should this company use?
explanation: AWS Enterprise Support provides a response time of 15 minutes for business-critical system down issues, which meets the company's requirement.

text: Which of the following AWS offerings are serverless services? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Lambda and Amazon DynamoDB are serverless services. AWS Lambda allows you to run code without provisioning or managing servers, and Amazon DynamoDB is a serverless NoSQL database.

text: Which AWS service enables you to quickly purchase and deploy SSL/TLS certificates?
explanation: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) allows you to provision, manage, and deploy public and private SSL/TLS certificates for use with AWS services and your internal resources. It simplifies the process of obtaining and renewing certificates.

text: Which AWS Service provides integration with Chef to automate the configuration of EC2 instances?
explanation: AWS OpsWorks is a configuration management service that provides the ability to automate the configuration of EC2 instances using Chef or Puppet.

text: A customer is seeking to store objects in their AWS environment and to make those objects downloadable over the internet. Which AWS Service can be used to accomplish this?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Amazon S3 is a highly scalable object storage service that allows you to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere on the web. It is designed for durability, availability, and cost-effectiveness, making it ideal for providing public or private access to objects.

text: Which of the following services can be used to monitor the HTTP and HTTPS requests that are forwarded to Amazon CloudFront?
explanation: AWS WAF is a web application firewall that can be used to monitor and filter HTTP/HTTPS requests forwarded to CloudFront, allowing you to protect against common web exploits.

text: A company is migrating a web application to AWS. The application's compute capacity is continually utilized throughout the year. Which of the below options offers the company the most cost-effective solution?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Reserved Instances offer significant discounts compared to On-Demand Instances when you commit to a term for instances with consistent utilization. Dedicated Hosts and Spot Instances are generally not the most cost-effective for continuously utilized workloads.

text: A company wants to grant a new employee long-term access to manage Amazon DynamoDB databases. Which of the following is a recommended best-practice when granting these permissions?
explanation: Creating an IAM user and attaching a policy with only the necessary Amazon DynamoDB access permissions follows the principle of least privilege, which is a crucial security best practice.

answers.text: Create an IAM role and attach a policy with Amazon DynamoDB access permissions.
Create an IAM role and attach a policy with Administrator access permissions.
Create an IAM user and attach a policy with Amazon DynamoDB access permissions.
Create an IAM user and attach a policy with Administrator access permissions.

text: When granting permissions to applications running on Amazon EC2 instances, which of the following is considered best practice?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Using IAM roles for EC2 instances provides temporary security credentials, which is a more secure and manageable approach than using long-term access keys embedded in application code or generated repeatedly.

answers.text: Generate new IAM access keys every time you delegate permissions.
Store the required AWS credentials directly within the application code.
Use temporary security credentials (IAM roles) instead of long-term access keys.
Do nothing; Applications that run on Amazon EC2 instances do not need permission to interact with other AWS services or resources.

text: Which of the following will help AWS customers save on costs when migrating their workloads to AWS?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Customers can leverage existing third-party software licenses on AWS, often through Bring Your Own License (BYOL) options, which can significantly reduce the cost of software acquisition during migration.

answers.text: Use servers instead of managed services.
Use existing third-party software licenses on AWS.
Migrate production workloads to AWS edge locations instead of AWS Regions.
Use AWS Outposts to run all workloads in a cost-optimized environment.

text: An organization has a legacy application designed using monolithic-based architecture. Which AWS Service can be used to decouple the components of the application?
explanation: Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a fully managed message queuing service that enables you to decouple and scale microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. Using SQS allows different components of an application to communicate asynchronously, which is key to decoupling a monolithic architecture.

text: Which of the following can be used to enable the Virtual Multi-Factor Authentication? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is used to manage access to AWS services and resources securely, including the configuration of multi-factor authentication (MFA). The AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) can be used to interact with IAM and manage MFA settings programmatically.

text: According to best practices, which of the below options is best suited for processing a large number of binary files?
explanation: Running EC2 instances in parallel allows for distributed processing of a large number of files, which is more efficient than vertically scaling a single instance for this type of workload.

text: A company is planning to use Amazon S3 and Amazon CloudFront to distribute its video courses globally. What tool can the company use to estimate the costs of these services?
explanation: The AWS Pricing Calculator is the designated tool for estimating the cost of AWS services, including Amazon S3 and Amazon CloudFront, based on projected usage.

text: What should you do if you see resources, which you don't remember creating, in the AWS Management Console? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Unrecognized resources may indicate a security breach. Changing root and IAM user passwords is a crucial immediate step to secure the account, and investigating logs (like CloudTrail) helps identify unauthorized access and potentially compromised users.

answers.text: Stop all running services and open an investigation.
Give your root account password to AWS Support so that they can assistin troubleshooting and securing the account.
Check the AWS CloudTrail logs and delete all IAM users that have access to your resources.
Open an investigation and delete any potentially compromised IAM users.
Change your AWS root account password and the passwords of any IAM users.

text: A key practice when designing solutions on AWS is to minimize dependencies between components so that the failure of a single component does not impact other components. What is this practice called?
explanation: Loosely coupling is a design principle that reduces dependencies between components, allowing them to be developed, deployed, and scaled independently. This makes systems more resilient and easier to manage.

text: Which AWS Service offers an NFS file system that can be mounted concurrently from multiple EC2 instances?
explanation: Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) provides a fully managed NFS file system that can be mounted concurrently from thousands of EC2 instances.

text: Availability Zones within a Region are connected over low-latency links. Which of the following is a benefit of these links?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Availability Zones are connected by low-latency, high-bandwidth links, which enables synchronous data replication between them, crucial for high availability and disaster recovery strategies.

answers.text: Create private connection to your data center.
Automate the process of provisioning new compute resources.
Make synchronous replication of your data possible.

text: Which of the following are true regarding the languages that are supported on AWS Lambda? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Lambda natively supports several programming languages, including Node.js, Python, and Java. It can also support any programming language by using custom runtimes via an API, allowing for greater flexibility.

answers.text: Lambda only supports Python and Node.js, but third party plugins are available to convert code in other languages to these formats.
Lambda natively supports a number of programming languages such as Node.js, Python, and Java.
Lambda is AWS' proprietary programming language for microservices.
Lambda doesn't support programming languages; it is a serverless compute service.
Lambda can support any programming language using an API.

text: What are the capabilities of AWS X-Ray? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS X-Ray helps you analyze and debug distributed applications by providing request tracing, which aids in identifying performance bottlenecks and application issues. By visualizing the flow of requests, it also helps in discovering performance improvements.

answers.text: Facilitates tracking of user requests to identify application issues.
Deploys applications to Amazon EC2 instances.

text: Which of the following is true regarding the AWS availability zones and edge locations?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Availability Zones are isolated locations within an AWS Region designed to be resilient to failures in one zone affecting others, while edge locations are globally distributed points of presence focused on content delivery and low-latency access.

answers.text: Edge locations are located in separate Availability Zones worldwide to serve global customers.
An availability zone exists within an edge location to distribute content globally with low latency.
An Availability Zone is a geographic location where AWS provides multiple, physically separated and isolated edge locations.
An AWS Availability Zone is an isolated location within an AWS Region, however edge locations are located in multiple cities worldwide.

text: Which features are included in the AWS Business Support Plan? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The AWS Business Support Plan offers 24x7 access to customer service and includes access to the Infrastructure Event Management (IEM) feature for an additional fee.

answers.text: Access to Cloud Support Engineers via email only during business hours.
Access to the Infrastructure Event Management (IEM) feature for additional fee.
Partial access to the core Trusted Advisor checks.

text: A company is developing a mobile application and wants to allow users to use their Amazon, Apple, Facebook, or Google identities to authenticate to the application. Which AWS Service should the company use for this purpose?
explanation: Amazon Cognito provides user identity and access management for your mobile and web applications. It allows users to sign in with their existing Facebook, Amazon, Google, or Apple credentials, or with a custom username and password.

text: Which AWS Service allows customers to create a template that programmatically defines policies and configurations of all AWS resources as code and so that the same template can be reused among multiple projects?
explanation: AWS CloudFormation is an infrastructure as code service that allows you to model and provision AWS and third-party application resources. AWS Config tracks resource configurations, AWS CloudTrail logs API calls, and AWS Auto Scaling adjusts capacity.

text: Which of the following are advantages of using AWS as a cloud computing provider? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS offers the advantage of eliminating the need to guess on infrastructure capacity by allowing users to scale resources up or down as needed. Additionally, it enables customers to trade capital expenses for operational expenses by moving from a pay-as-you-go model for computing resources.

answers.text: Eliminates the need to monitor servers and applications.
Manages all the compliance and auditing tasks.
Provides custom hardware to meet any specification.
Eliminates the need to guess on infrastructure capacity needs.
Enables customers to trade their capital expenses for operational expenses.

text: A customer is planning to migrate their Microsoft SQL Server databases to AWS. Which AWS Services can the customer use to run their Microsoft SQL Server database on AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon RDS and Amazon EC2 are the primary services that allow customers to run Microsoft SQL Server databases on AWS. Amazon RDS offers a managed database service, while EC2 provides virtual servers where SQL Server can be installed and managed manually.

text: Which AWS Service can perform health checks on Amazon EC2 instances?
explanation: Amazon Route 53 performs health checks on Amazon EC2 instances to ensure they are available and responding correctly. If an instance fails a health check, Route 53 can stop sending traffic to it.

text: A company is developing an application that will leverage facial recognition to automate photo tagging. Which AWS Service should the company use for facial recognition?
explanation: Amazon Rekognition is an AWS service that analyzes images and videos using machine learning for tasks such as facial recognition, object detection, and content moderation.

text: Which of the following are examples of AWS-managed databases? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS-managed databases are services where AWS handles the underlying infrastructure, patching, backups, and scaling. Amazon Neptune and Amazon RDS for MySQL are both fully managed database services offered by AWS.

answers.text: Microsoft SQL Server on Amazon EC2.

text: A company's AWS workflow requires that it periodically perform large-scale image and video processing jobs. The customer is seeking to minimize cost and has stated that the amount of time it takes to process these jobs is not critical, but that cost minimization is the most important factor in designing the solution. Which EC2 instance class is best suited for this processing?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: EC2 Spot Instances offer the most significant cost savings among EC2 instance types, making them ideal for fault-tolerant workloads where interruptions are acceptable, such as large-scale image and video processing jobs that are not time-critical.

text: There is a requirement to grant a DevOps team full administrative access to all resources in an AWS account. Who can grant them these permissions?
explanation: The AWS account owner is the ultimate authority for managing access and permissions within their AWS account. They can grant administrative privileges to users or groups using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM).

text: You need to migrate a large number of on-premises workloads to AWS. Which AWS service is the most appropriate?
explanation: AWS Server Migration Service (SMS) is designed to facilitate the migration of on-premises virtual machines to AWS, making it suitable for large-scale workload migrations. While Application Discovery Service helps in assessing workloads, it's not the migration service itself.

text: What are some key benefits of using AWS CloudFormation? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS CloudFormation allows you to model and provision your entire infrastructure using a text file, enabling automation of infrastructure deployment and updates in a safe and controlled manner.

answers.text: It helps AWS customers deploy their applications without worrying about the underlying infrastructure.
It applies advanced IAM security features automatically.
It automates the provisioning and updating of your infrastructure in a safe and controlled manner.
It allows you to model your entire infrastructure in just a text file.
It compiles and builds application code in a timely manner.

text: Which of the following is a cloud computing deployment model that connects infrastructure and applications between cloud-based resources and existing resources not located in the cloud?
explanation: A hybrid cloud is a computing environment that combines on-premises infrastructure, private cloud storage, and/or public cloud services, allowing data and applications to be shared between them.

text: A company is hosting business critical workloads in an AWS Region. To protect against data loss and ensure business continuity, a mirror image of the current AWS environment should be created in another AWS Region. Company policy requires that the standby environment must be available in minutes in case of an outage in the primary AWS Region. Which AWS service can be used to meet these requirements?
explanation: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (AWS DRS) is designed to provide continuous, block-level replication, enabling you to recover applications in minutes. CloudEndure Migration is for migrating data, AWS Backup is for backup and restore, and AWS Glue is for data integration.

text: Which of the following S3 storage classes is most appropriate to host static assets for a popular e-commerce website with stable access patterns?
explanation: S3 Standard is ideal for hosting static assets with frequent access and stable patterns, offering low latency and high throughput required for popular e-commerce websites.

text: You want to create a backup of your data in another geographical location. Where should you create this backup?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Regions are geographically distinct areas, allowing you to create backups in separate locations for disaster recovery and compliance. Availability Zones are within a Region, and VPCs are isolated network environments, neither providing the geographical separation needed.

text: Which statement is true in relation to the security of Amazon EC2?
explanation: AWS operates on a shared responsibility model. While AWS secures the underlying infrastructure, customers are responsible for securing their operating systems and applications running on EC2 instances, including patching the OS and applications.

answers.text: You should use instance store volumes to store login data.
You should regularly patch the operating system and applications on your EC2 instances.
You should deploy critical components of your application in the Availability Zone that you trust.
You can track all API calls using Amazon Athena.

text: What does AWS Cost Explorer provide to help manage your AWS spend?
explanation: AWS Cost Explorer allows you to visualize, understand, and manage your AWS costs and usage over time, including providing highly accurate cost forecasts for up to 12 months ahead.

answers.text: Cost comparisons between AWS Cloud environments and on-premises environments.
Accurate estimates of AWS service costs based on your expected usage.
Highly accurate cost forecasts for up to 12 months ahead.

text: Which of the following is a feature of Amazon RDS that performs automatic failover when the primary database fails to respond?
explanation: Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployments provide enhanced availability by automatically failing over to a standby replica in a different Availability Zone in the event of an infrastructure failure.

text: You are using several on-demand EC2 Instances to run your development environment. What is the best way to reduce your charges when these instances are not in use?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Stopping an EC2 instance while keeping the attached EBS volumes allows you to reduce charges because you only pay for the EBS storage, not for the running instance compute time. Terminating instances permanently deletes them and their root EBS volumes, while deleting EBS volumes alone does not stop instance charges if the instance is still running.

answers.text: Deleting all EBS volumes attached to the instances.
You cannot minimize charges for on-demand instances.

text: Which of the following strategies helps protect your AWS root account?
explanation: Protecting the root account is critical. Deleting unused root user access keys reduces the attack surface by removing credentials that could be compromised.

answers.text: Delete root user access keys if you do not need them.
Apply MFA for the root account and use it for all of your work.
Access the root account only from your personal Mobile Phone.
Only share your AWS account password or access keys with trusted persons.

text: Which of the following are factors should be considered for Amazon EBS pricing? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon EBS pricing is influenced by the size of the volumes provisioned per month and the amount of data stored in snapshots. Other factors like compute capacity, compute time, or Snowball devices are not directly associated with EBS pricing.

answers.text: The size of volumes provisioned per month.
The amount of data you have stored in snapshots.
The number of Snowball storage devices you request.

text: You have just set up your AWS environment and have created six IAM user accounts for the DevOps team. What is the AWS recommendation when granting permissions to these IAM accounts?
explanation: The AWS recommendation is to apply the Principle of Least Privilege, which means granting users only the minimum permissions necessary to perform their tasks.

answers.text: Attach a separate IAM policy for each individual account.
Apply the Principle of Least Privilege.
For security purposes, you should not grant any permission to the DevOps team.

text: Which of the following has the greatest impact on cost? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Compute charges, such as for Amazon EC2 instances, are typically a significant portion of cloud spending. Data Transfer Out charges, which incur costs when data leaves AWS regions, can also become very expensive, especially with high volumes.

answers.text: The number of IAM roles provisioned.

text: Who from the following will get the largest discount?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Customers receive the largest discount on Amazon EC2 instances when they purchase Reserved Instances with a Standard RI type and an All Upfront payment option, as this commitment provides the highest level of cost savings.

answers.text: A user who chooses to buy On-demand, Convertible, Partial upfront instances.
A user who chooses to buy Reserved, Convertible, All upfront instances.
A user who chooses to buy Reserved, Standard, No upfront instances.
A user who chooses to buy Reserved, Standard, All upfront instances.

text: Which of the following is an available option when purchasing Amazon EC2 instances?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Purchasing On-Demand Instances, Reserved Instances, or Savings Plans allows customers to pay upfront to receive lower hourly costs for their EC2 usage.

answers.text: The ability to bid to get the lowest possible prices.
The ability to register EC2 instances to get volume discounts on every hour the instances are running.
The ability to buy Dedicated Instances for up to 90% discount.
The ability to pay upfront to get lower hourly costs.

text: What does the term 'Economies of scale' mean?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Economies of scale in the AWS Cloud context means that as AWS consumes resources at a massive scale, it can offer those resources to customers at lower prices, and these cost savings are often passed on.

answers.text: It means that you save more when you consume more.
It means as more time passes using AWS, you pay more for its services.
It means that AWS will continuously lower costs as it grows.
It means that you have the ability to pay as you go.

text: A company experiences fluctuations in traffic patterns to their e-commerce website when running flash sales. What service can help the company dynamically match the required compute capacity to handle spikes in traffic during flash sales?
explanation: AWS Auto Scaling can dynamically adjust the number of Amazon EC2 instances to match the required compute capacity, enabling it to handle traffic spikes during flash sales.

text: Which of the below options is true of Amazon VPC?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS customers have complete control over their Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) virtual networking environment, including their IP address range, subnets, route tables, and network gateways.

answers.text: Amazon VPC allows customers to control user interactions with all other AWS resources.
AWS Customers have complete control over their Amazon VPC virtual networking environment.
AWS is responsible for all the management and configuration details of Amazon VPC.
Amazon VPC helps customers to review their AWS architecture and adopt best practices.

text: Which tool can a non-AWS customer use to compare the cost of on-premises environment resources to AWS?
explanation: The AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator helps estimate the cost savings of migrating to the AWS Cloud. It allows users to compare the costs of their on-premises IT infrastructure with AWS cloud infrastructure.

text: Which of the following services provide real-time auditing for compliance and vulnerabilities? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Config provides an up-to-date inventory of your AWS resources, their configurations, and their relationships, enabling compliance auditing. AWS Trusted Advisor checks your AWS environment for ways to reduce costs, improve performance, increase security, and identify potential vulnerabilities.

text: Which of the following AWS services uses Puppet to automate how EC2 instances are configured?
explanation: AWS OpsWorks is a configuration management service that uses automation tools like Puppet or Chef to configure and manage EC2 instances. AWS CloudFormation and AWS Quick Starts use templates, and AWS CloudTrail is for logging API calls.

text: An organization uses a hybrid cloud architecture to run their business. Which AWS service enables them to deploy their applications to any AWS or on-premises server?
explanation: AWS CodeDeploy is a service that automates code deployments to various compute services, including on-premises servers, making it suitable for hybrid cloud architectures.

text: Select the services that are server-based: (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon RDS and Amazon EMR are both server-based services, meaning AWS manages the underlying servers for you. AWS Lambda is serverless, and AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers.

text: What best describes penetration testing?
explanation: Penetration testing, often referred to as pen testing, is an authorized simulated cyberattack on a computer system, performed to evaluate the security of the system. It is designed to identify security vulnerabilities in the network that an attacker could exploit.

answers.text: Testing your application's response time from different locations.
Testing your network to find security vulnerabilities that an attacker could exploit.
Testing your instances to check for the unhealthy ones.
Testing your software for bugs and errors.

text: Which of the following are use cases for Amazon EMR? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon EMR is designed for analyzing and processing large datasets in a timely manner and easily running and scaling big data frameworks like Apache Spark and Hadoop.

answers.text: Enables you to backup extremely large amounts of data at very low costs.
Enables you to move Exabyte-scale data from on-premises datacenters into AWS.
Enables you to analyze and process extremely large amounts of data in a timely manner.
Enables you to easily run and scale Apache Spark, Hadoop,and other Big Data frameworks.
Enables you to easily run and manage Docker containers.

text: Your CTO has asked you to contact AWS support using the chat feature to ask for guidance related to EBS. However, when you open the AWS support center you can't see a way to contact support via Chat. What should you do?
explanation: The chat feature for AWS support is only available for the Business and Enterprise support plans. Therefore, you must upgrade to at least the Business support plan to access this feature.

answers.text: There is no chat feature in AWS support.
The chat feature is available for all plans for an additional fee, but you have to request it first.
At a minimum, upgrade to Business support plan.
Upgrade from the Basic Support plan to Developer Support.

text: A developer wants to quickly deploy and manage his application in the AWS Cloud, but he doesn't have any experience with cloud computing. Which of the following AWS services would help the developer achieve his goal?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a managed service that automatically handles the deployment, capacity provisioning, load balancing, auto-scaling, and application health monitoring of applications, making it ideal for developers new to the cloud.

text: Which statement best describes the AWS Pay-As-You-Go pricing model?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: The AWS Pay-As-You-Go model allows customers to pay only for the compute power, storage, and other resources they consume, effectively replacing large upfront capital expenses with low variable payments.

answers.text: With AWS, you replace low upfront expenses with large variable payments.
With AWS, you replace low upfront expenses with large fixed payments.
With AWS, you replace large upfront expenses with low fixed payments.
With AWS, you replace large capital expenses with low variable payments.

text: For Amazon RDS databases, what does AWS perform on your behalf? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS performs database setup and manages the operating system for Amazon RDS databases, alleviating these tasks from the user.

text: Which of the following strategies help analyze costs in AWS?
explanation: Using tags to group resources allows you to categorize and track costs associated with specific projects, environments, or teams, which is a fundamental strategy for cost analysis in AWS.

answers.text: Using AWS CloudFormation to automate the deployment of resources.
Deploying resources of the same type in different regions.
Configuring Amazon Inspector to automatically analyze costs and email reports.

text: A media company has an application that requires the transfer of large data sets to and from AWS every day. This data is business critical and should be transferred over a consistent connection. Which AWS service should the company use?
explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides a dedicated, consistent network connection from an on-premises environment to AWS, suitable for large, business-critical data transfers.

text: What is the main benefit of the AWS Storage Gateway service?
explanation: AWS Storage Gateway provides a hybrid cloud storage solution that connects an on-premises software appliance with cloud-based storage. This allows on-premises applications to seamlessly access scalable, secure, and cost-effective cloud storage.

answers.text: It automates the process of building, maintaining, and running ETL jobs.
It provides physical devices to migrate data from on premises to AWS.
It allows integration of on-premises IT environments with Cloud Storage.
It provides hardware-based key storage for regulatory compliance.

text: To protect against data loss, you need to backup your database regularly. What is the most cost-effective storage option that provides immediate retrieval of your backups?
explanation: Amazon S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) is the most cost-effective storage class for data that is accessed less frequently but requires rapid access when needed, making it suitable for regular database backups.

text: Which service can you use to route traffic to the endpoint that provides the best application performance for your users worldwide?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Global Accelerator provides static IP addresses that act as a fixed entry point and use the AWS global network to route user traffic to the healthiest and nearest AWS endpoint, thereby improving application performance.

text: Why are Serverless Architectures more economical than Server-based Architectures?
explanation: Serverless architectures are more economical because you pay only for the compute time consumed when your code is executing, unlike server-based architectures where resources might run continuously. This pay-per-execution model significantly reduces costs for workloads with variable or infrequent demand.

answers.text: Serverless Architectures use new powerful computing devices.
With the Server-based Architectures, compute resources continue to run all the time but with serverless architecture, compute resources are only used when code is being executed.
When you reserve serverless capacity, you will get large discounts compared to server reservation.
With Serverless Architectures you have the ability to scale automatically up or down as demand changes.

text: Which of the below options are use cases of the Amazon Route 53 service? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon Route 53 is a highly available and scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service that is used for DNS configuration and management, and for managing global application traffic through a variety of routing types.

answers.text: Point-to-point connectivity between an on-premises data center and AWS.
Detects configuration changes in the AWS environment.
Manages global application traffic through a variety of routing types.

text: You want to transfer 200 Terabytes of data from on-premises locations to the AWS Cloud, which of the following can do the job in a cost-effective way?
explanation: AWS Snowball is designed for transferring large amounts of data into and out of AWS. For 200 TB, it is a cost-effective solution compared to high-speed internet transfer.

text: You have a real-time IoT application that requires sub-millisecond latency. Which of the following services should you use?
explanation: Amazon ElastiCache for Redis is an in-memory data store that can provide sub-millisecond latency for real-time applications.

text: Which of the following can help secure your sensitive data in Amazon S3? (Choose TWO)
explanation: To secure sensitive data in Amazon S3, you should enable S3 encryption features like server-side encryption or client-side encryption. Additionally, encrypting the data before uploading it to S3 provides an extra layer of security.

answers.text: Delete the encryption keys once your data is encrypted.
With AWS you do not need to worry about encryption.
Encrypt the data prior to uploading it.
Delete all IAM users that have access to S3.

text: Which AWS service helps developers compile and test their code?
explanation: AWS CodeBuild is a fully managed continuous integration service that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces software packages that are ready to deploy.

text: Which of the following will affect how much you are charged for storing objects in S3? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon S3 charges are based on the total size of objects stored and the storage class used, which determines factors like durability and access frequency.

answers.text: Using default encryption for any number of S3 buckets.
The number of EBS volumes attached to your instances.
The storage class used for the objects stored.
The total size in gigabytes of all objects stored.

text: What does the Amazon CloudFront service provide? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a global content delivery network (CDN) that securely delivers content, static, dynamic, and streaming, with low latency. It caches content at edge locations worldwide, increasing application availability and improving performance for end users.

answers.text: Tracks user activity and APl usage.
Increases application availability by caching at the edge.
Stores archived data at very low costs.
Delivers content to end users with low latency.

text: You are facing a lot of problems with your current contact center. Which service provides a cloud-based contact center that can deliver a better service for your customers?
explanation: Amazon Connect is a cloud-based contact center service that helps companies provide better customer service. It offers advanced features and a pay-as-you-go pricing model.

text: You have migrated your application to AWS recently. How can you view the AWS costs applied to your account?
explanation: The AWS Cost & Usage Report (CUR) provides the most comprehensive dataset on your AWS costs and usage. It breaks down costs by account, Linked Account, service, usage type, and tags, offering granular insights into your spending.

answers.text: Using the AWS Cost & Usage Report.
Using the AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) dashboard.
Using the AWS CloudWatch logs dashboard.

text: Which of the following are valid Amazon EC2 Reserved Instance types? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Amazon EC2 Reserved Instances offer two types: Standard and Convertible. Standard Reserved Instances provide a significant discount compared to On-Demand Instances, while Convertible Reserved Instances offer flexibility to change the instance attributes.

text: Which of the following services gives you access to all AWS auditor-issued reports and certifications?
explanation: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS security and compliance reports and certifications maintained by AWS and its auditors. AWS Config, Amazon CloudWatch, and AWS CloudTrail are operational and monitoring services, not repository for compliance reports.

text: You manage a blog on AWS that has different environments: development, testing, and production. What can you use to create a custom console for each environment to view and manage your resources easily?
explanation: AWS Resource Groups allow you to group resources together based on tags or specific criteria, enabling you to create custom views and consoles for managing resources in different environments like development, testing, and production.

text: Which AWS service collects metrics from running EC2 instances?
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is the service that collects monitoring and operational data in the form of logs, metrics, and events from AWS services, including EC2 instances. This data can then be used for analysis and debugging.

text: Your web application currently faces performance issues and suffers from long load times. Which of the following AWS services could help fix these issues and improve performance?
explanation: AWS X-Ray helps you analyze and debug distributed applications, such as those built using a microservices architecture. It provides an end-to-end view of requests as they travel through your application, highlighting performance bottlenecks and errors.

text: Which of the following compute resources are serverless? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Lambda and AWS Fargate are serverless compute options, meaning you do not need to provision or manage servers. You only pay for the compute time you consume.

text: For compliance and regulatory purposes, a government agency requires that their applications must run on hardware that is dedicated to them only. How can you meet this requirement?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: EC2 Dedicated Hosts provide physical servers with EC2 instance capacity that are dedicated to your use, satisfying the requirement for dedicated hardware for compliance and regulatory purposes.

text: Which AWS Cost Governance best practice recommends refining workloads regularly to make the most of existing AWS resources and reduce costs?
explanation: Architecture Optimization is the best practice that focuses on refining workloads to maximize the use of AWS resources and thus reduce costs.

text: An organization needs to build a financial application that requires support for ACID transactions. Which AWS database service is most appropriate in this case?
explanation: Amazon RDS supports ACID transactions, which are essential for financial applications requiring data integrity and reliability. Redshift is a data warehousing service, CloudHSM is for cryptographic key management, and DMS is for database migration.

text: What can you use to assign permissions directly to an IAM user?
explanation: IAM policies define permissions. These policies can be attached directly to an IAM user to grant specific permissions to that user.

text: The owner of an E-Commerce application notices that the compute capacity requirements vary heavily from time to time. What makes AWS more economical than traditional data centers for this type of application?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS offers elasticity, allowing you to provision and de-provision EC2 instances dynamically based on demand. This pay-as-you-go model for compute capacity is more economical than maintaining over-provisioned hardware in traditional data centers to handle peak loads.

answers.text: AWS allows customers to launch powerful EC2 instances to handle spikes in load.
AWS allows customers to pay upfront to get bigger discounts.
AWS allows customers to launch and terminate EC2 instances based on demand.
AWS allows customers to choose cheaper types of EC2 instances that best fit their needs.

text: Amazon RDS supports multiple database engines to choose from. Which of the following is not one of them?
explanation: Amazon RDS supports PostgreSQL, Oracle, and Microsoft SQL Server, but it does not natively support Teradata. Teradata is a separate commercial data warehousing system.

text: Which of the following AWS services would help you migrate on-premise databases to AWS?
explanation: AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) is specifically designed to help migrate databases from on-premises to AWS easily and securely. It supports homogenous and heterogeneous migrations between different database sources and targets.

text: For new AWS customers, what is the EASIEST way to launch a simple WordPress website on AWS?
explanation: Amazon Lightsail offers pre-configured instances with WordPress already installed, making it the simplest option for new AWS customers to launch a website. While EC2 can host WordPress, it requires more manual setup, and S3 is for static website hosting.

answers.text: Run WordPress on an Amazon Lightsail instance.
Install WordPress on an Amazon EC2 instance.
Use the Amazon S3 Web hosting feature.
Host the website directly on AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK).

text: Which of the following would you use to manage your encryption keys in the AWS Cloud? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) and CloudHSM are services designed to manage encryption keys, providing secure key storage and cryptographic operations for your AWS resources.

text: Which of the following services allows you to install and run custom relational database software?
explanation: Amazon EC2 allows you to install and run any software, including custom relational database software, on virtual servers in the cloud. Amazon RDS is a managed service that only supports specific database engines.

text: Your application requirements for CPU and RAM are changing in an unpredictable way. Which service can be used to dynamically adjust these resources based on load?
explanation: AWS Auto Scaling dynamically adjusts compute resources, such as Amazon EC2 instances, based on demand. This ensures your application has the right amount of resources to handle traffic fluctuations without manual intervention.

text: A company has infrastructure hosted in an on-premises data center. They currently have an operations team that takes care of identity management. If they decide to migrate to the AWS cloud, which of the following services would help them perform the same role in AWS?
explanation: AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management) is the service used to manage access to AWS services and resources securely. It allows you to create and manage AWS users and groups, and control what they can and cannot do in your AWS account.

text: What are some key design principles for designing public cloud systems? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Loose coupling increases resilience and scalability by allowing components to function independently, while disposable resources promote automation and reduce the impact of failures by treating infrastructure as ephemeral.

answers.text: Reserved capacity instead of on demand.
Disposable resources instead of fixed servers.
Multi-AZ deployments instead of multi-region deployments.

text: Where can AWS account owners get a list of all users in their account, including the status of their AWS credentials?
explanation: The IAM Credential Report provides a granular view of IAM users, their access keys, MFA status, and password last used date, offering a comprehensive overview of credential status.

text: Which of the following services enables you to easily generate and use your own encryption keys in the AWS Cloud?
explanation: AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated hardware security modules (HSMs) in the cloud, allowing you to generate and manage your own encryption keys.

text: You have developed a web application targeting a global audience. Which of the following will help you achieve the highest redundancy and fault tolerance from an infrastructure perspective?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Deploying your application in multiple Availability Zones across multiple AWS Regions provides the highest level of redundancy and fault tolerance by distributing your application across geographically separate locations and independent data centers.

answers.text: There is no need to architect for these capabilities in AWS, as AWS is redundant by default.
Deploy the application in a single Availability Zone.
Deploy the application in multiple Availability Zones in a single AWS region.
Deploy the application in multiple Availability Zones in multiple AWS regions.

text: For some services, AWS automatically replicates data across multiple Availability Zones to provide fault tolerance in the event of a server failure or Availability Zone outage. Select TWO services that automatically replicate data across Availability Zones.
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
