explanation: Amazon S3 and Amazon DynamoDB are designed for high availability and durability, automatically replicating data across multiple Availability Zones within an AWS Region to protect against failures.

text: Which of the following factors affect Amazon CloudFront cost? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon CloudFront costs are primarily affected by the number of requests made and the amount of data transferred out from edge locations to users. Inbound data transfer and requests to origin servers are generally free.

text: Which of the following resources can an AWS customer use to learn more about prohibited uses of the services offered by AWS?
explanation: The AWS Acceptable Use Policy explicitly outlines the prohibited uses of AWS services. AWS Service Control Policies are for managing permissions, AWS Artifact provides compliance reports, and AWS Budgets is for cost management.

text: Which of the following security resources are available to any user for free? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Bulletins and the AWS Security Blog are publicly available resources that provide information about AWS security. AWS TAM (Technical Account Manager) is a paid service, and AWS Support API and Classroom Training are typically associated with paid support plans or specific training programs.

text: How can you protect data stored on Amazon S3 from accidental deletion?
explanation: Enabling S3 Versioning protects data by maintaining multiple versions of an object in the same bucket, allowing you to restore previous versions if accidental deletions occur.

text: Which of the following is the responsibility of AWS according to the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security OF the cloud, which includes protecting the infrastructure that runs all AWS services, such as regions and edge locations. Customers are responsible for security IN the cloud, which includes content, applications, and data.

text: Which of the following AWS support plans provides access to only the seven core AWS Trusted Advisor checks?
explanation: The Developer and Basic support plans include access to the seven core AWS Trusted Advisor checks to help you identify opportunities to optimize your AWS environment.

text: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of using AWS Lambda?
explanation: AWS Lambda provides resizable compute capacity for your code. The other options describe benefits, but Lambda doesn't directly offer the ability to be called from any mobile app without additional services.

answers.text: AWS Lambda runs code without provisioning or managing servers.
AWS Lambda provides resizable compute capacity in the cloud.
There is no charge when your AWS Lambda code is not running.
AWS Lambda can be called directly from any mobile app.

text: How does AWS help customers achieve compliance in the cloud?
explanation: AWS regularly has its services assessed to meet various compliance requirements and certifications, offering a strong foundation for customers to build compliant solutions.

answers.text: It's not possible to meet regulatory compliance requirements in the Cloud.
AWS applies the most common Cloud security standards, and is responsible for complying with customers' applicable laws and regulations.
AWS has many common assurance certifications such as ISO 9001 and HIPAA.
Many AWS services are assessed regularly to comply with local laws and regulations.

text: Who is responsible for scaling a DynamoDB database in the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?
explanation: AWS is responsible for the underlying infrastructure that enables scaling of DynamoDB. While customers configure Auto Scaling settings, AWS manages the actual scaling of the database instances.

text: You are working as a web app developer. You are currently facing issues in media playback for mobile devices because your media format is not supported. Which of the following AWS services can help you convert your media into another format?
explanation: Amazon Elastic Transcoder is a media transcoding service that is used to convert media files into formats that will play on a wide range of devices. Amazon S3 is object storage, and Amazon Rekognition is for image and video analysis; neither directly performs media format conversion.

text: What are the benefits of the AWS Organizations service? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Organizations enables you to centrally manage and govern your environment as you grow and scale your AWS resources by controlling access to AWS services and consolidating billing across multiple AWS accounts.

answers.text: Help organizations design and maintain an accelerated path to successful cloud adoption.
Help organization achieve their desired business outcomes with AWS.
Consolidate billing across multiple AWS accounts.

text: Which AWS service allows you to build a data warehouse in the cloud?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon Redshift is a fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service in the cloud. It is optimized for high-performance analysis and retrieval of large datasets.

text: What AWS service allows you to buy third-party software solutions and services that run on AWS resources?
explanation: AWS Marketplace is a digital catalog where you can find, buy, deploy, and manage third-party software, data, and services that are optimized to run on AWS.

text: Which of the following services is an AWS repository management system that allows for storing, versioning, and managing your application code?
explanation: AWS CodeCommit is a fully managed source control service that hosts secure Git-based repositories, making it ideal for storing, versioning, and managing application code.

text: Which AWS service can be used to route end users to the nearest AWS Region to reduce latency?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon Route 53 is a scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service. It provides reliable and cost-efficient way to route end users to applications by translating human-readable domain names that people access every day into the numeric IP addresses that computers use to connect to each other. Route 53 provides several routing types, including latency-based routing, which routes users to the AWS Region that provides the lowest latency.

text: Which feature enables users to sign into their AWS accounts with their existing corporate credentials?
explanation: Federation allows users to authenticate with AWS using their existing corporate identities, such as Active Directory, which streamlines access and enhances security by centralizing user management.

text: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, what are the controls that customers fully inherit from AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security of the cloud, which includes physical data center security and environmental controls that customers fully inherit. Customers are responsible for security in the cloud, such as configuring resources and managing access.

text: What can you access by visiting the URL: http://status.aws.amazon.com?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: The URL http://status.aws.amazon.com directs users to the AWS Service Health Dashboard, which provides information about the operational status of AWS services.

text: Which of the following procedures can reduce latency when your end users are retrieving data? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Storing media assets in the AWS Region closest to end users and using Amazon CloudFront to cache and distribute assets reduces the physical distance data must travel, thereby lowering latency.

answers.text: Store media assets in the region closest to your end users.
Store media assets on an additional EBS volume and increase the capacity of your server.
Replicate media assets to at least two availability zones.
Reduce the size of media assets using the Amazon Elastic Transcoder.
Store media assets in S3 and use CloudFront to distribute these assets.

text: Which of the following are part of the seven design principles for security in the cloud? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Two key security design principles are to enable real-time traceability for continuous monitoring and detection, and to use IAM roles for temporary credentials rather than long-term ones to minimize exposure.

answers.text: Use manual monitoring techniques to protect your AWS resources.
Use IAM roles to grant temporary access instead of long-term credentials.
Scale horizontally to protect from failures.
Never store sensitive data in the cloud.

text: A company is migrating production workloads to AWS, and they are concerned about cost management across different departments. Which option should the company implement to categorize and track AWS spending?
explanation: Applying cost allocation tags to resources allows you to segment and categorize your AWS costs by different projects, departments, or other logical divisions. This enables granular tracking and analysis of spending.

answers.text: Use the AWS Pricing Calculator service to monitor the costs incurred by each department.
Use Amazon Aurora to forecast AWS spending based on usage.
Apply cost allocation tags to segment AWS costs by different projects and departments.
Configure AWS Price List API to receive billing updates for each department automatically.

text: What is the main benefit of attaching security groups to an Amazon RDS instance?
explanation: Security groups act as virtual firewalls for your RDS instances, controlling inbound and outbound traffic. They allow you to specify which IP address ranges or other security groups can connect to your database instance.

answers.text: Manages user access and encryption keys.
Controls what IP address ranges can connect to your database instance.
Deploys SSL/TLS certificates for use with your database instance.
Distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets.

text: A company wants to use Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) to run its containerized applications. For compliance reasons, the company wants to retain complete visibility and control over the underlying server cluster. Which Amazon ECS launch type will satisfy these requirements?
explanation: The EC2 launch type allows you to manage the underlying EC2 instances that your containers run on, providing the necessary visibility and control for compliance. Fargate abstracts away the underlying infrastructure.

text: You have multiple standalone AWS accounts and you want to decrease your AWS monthly charges. What should you do?
explanation: Adding multiple AWS accounts to an AWS Organization enables Consolidated Billing, which allows you to combine usage across accounts to take advantage of volume discounts and pay actual usage, thereby reducing overall costs.

answers.text: Try to remove unnecessary AWS accounts.
Add the accounts to an AWS Organization and use Consolidated Billing.
Track the AWS charges that are incurred by the member accounts.
Enable AWS tiered-pricing before provisioning resources.

text: You have been tasked with auditing the security of your VPC. As part of this process, you need to start by analyzing what inbound and outbound traffic is allowed on your EC2 instances. What two parts of the VPC do you need to check to accomplish this task?
explanation: Security Groups act as virtual firewalls for your EC2 instances, controlling inbound and outbound traffic at the instance level. Network Access Control Lists (NACLs) provide a stateless layer of security, controlling traffic at the subnet level.

text: What does the AWS 'Business' support plan provide? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The AWS Business support plan provides access to the full set of AWS Trusted Advisor checks, which help customers optimize their AWS environment. It also grants access to the AWS Support API, enabling programmatic interaction with AWS Support.

answers.text: Access to the full set of Trusted Advisor checks.
Less than 15 minutes response-time support if your business critical system goes down.

text: You have just finished writing your application code. Which service can be used to automate the deployment and scaling of your application?
explanation: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a fully managed service that provides an easy way to deploy and scale web applications and services developed with Java, .NET, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, and Docker on familiar servers such as Apache, Nginx, Passenger, and IIS.

text: Which statement is true in relation to security in AWS?
explanation: AWS manages the security of the cloud infrastructure, while customers are responsible for security *in* the cloud. This includes patching operating systems and database software when running on EC2 instances.

answers.text: AWS manages everything related to EC2 operating systems.
AWS customers are responsible for patching any database software running on Amazon EC2.
Server side encryption is the responsibility of AWS.
AWS is responsible for the security of your application.

text: Amazon EC2 instances are conceptually very similar to traditional servers. However, using Amazon EC2 server instances in the same manner as traditional hardware server instances is only a starting point. What are the main benefits of using the AWS EC2 instances instead of traditional servers? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: AWS EC2 instances improve fault tolerance through features like Availability Zones and Auto Scaling. They can be manually scaled in a shorter period than traditional servers by adjusting instance types and counts.

answers.text: Provides your business with a seamless remote accessibility.
Prevents unauthorized users from getting into your network.
Can be scaled manually in a shorter period of time.

text: Which statement is true regarding AWS pricing? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS offers a pay-as-you-go pricing model, meaning you pay only for the services you consume without any upfront fees or long-term contracts. This provides flexibility and cost efficiency.

answers.text: With the AWS pay-as-you-go pricing model, you don't have to pay any upfront fee.
You have no responsibility for third-party software license costs.
You only pay for the individual services that you need with no long-term contracts.
For some services, you have to pay a startup fee in order to get the service running.
There are no reservations on AWS, you only pay for what you use.

text: Which AWS service provides the EASIEST way to set up and manage a secure, well-architected, multi-account AWS environment?
explanation: AWS Control Tower provides the easiest way to set up and manage a secure, multi-account AWS environment by automating the creation of landing zones and enforcing governance policies.

answers.text: AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager AWS Security Hub.

text: A company is running a large web application that needs to always be available. The application tends to slow down when CPU usage is greater than 60%. How can they track when CPU usage goes above 60% for any of the EC2 Instances in their account?
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch Alarms can be configured to monitor EC2 instance metrics, such as CPU utilization, and trigger an alarm when a defined threshold is breached, such as exceeding 60%. SNS can be used in conjunction with CloudWatch alarms to send notifications, but CloudWatch is the primary service for monitoring and alarming on metrics.

answers.text: Use CloudFront to monitor the CPU usage.
Set the AWS Config CPU threshold to 60% to receive a notification when EC2 usage exceeds that value.
Use CloudWatch Alarms to monitor the CPUand alert when the CPU usage is >= 60%.
Use SNS to menitor the utilization of the server.

text: What is the recommended storage option when hosting an often-changing database on an Amazon EC2 instance?
explanation: Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) provides persistent block storage volumes for use with Amazon EC2 instances, making it suitable for frequently changing data like databases.

answers.text: You can't run a database inside an Amazon EC2 instance.

text: You are working as a site reliability engineer (SRE) in an AWS environment, which of the following services helps monitor your applications?
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and observability service built for DevOps engineers, developers, site reliability engineers, and IT managers. It provides data and actionable insights to monitor applications, respond to changes in your applications, understand and react to your operating system, and manage your AWS resources.

text: What factors determine how you are charged when using AWS Lambda? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Lambda charges are based on the number of requests for your function and the compute time consumed, measured in milliseconds.

answers.text: Number of requests to your functions.

text: What are the main differences between an IAM user and an IAM role in AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: IAM users are directly associated with individuals and have permanent credentials, while IAM roles are designed to be assumed by users, applications, or services and provide temporary credentials.

answers.text: An IAM user is uniquely associated with only one person, however a role is intended to be assumable by anyone who needs it.
An IAM user has permanent credentials associated with it, however a role has temporary credentials associated with it.
IAM users are more cost effective than IAM roles.
Arole is uniquely associated with only one person, however an IAM user is intended to be assumable by anyone who needs it.
An IAM user has temporary credentials associated withit, however a role has permanent credentials associated with it.

text: Which of the following actions may reduce Amazon EBS costs? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Deleting unnecessary EBS snapshots frees up storage space and reduces associated costs. Changing the EBS volume type to a more cost-effective option that meets performance needs can also lower expenses.

answers.text: Changing the type of the volume.

text: What does Amazon GuardDuty do to protect AWS accounts and workloads?
explanation: Amazon GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors AWS accounts and workloads for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior. It helps identify threats such as reconnaissance, account compromise, and resource deployment by attackers.

answers.text: Notifies AWS customers about abuse events once they are reported.
Continuously monitors AWS infrastructure and helps detect threats such as attacker reconnaissance or account compromise.
Helps AWS customers identify the root cause of potential security issues.
Checks security groups for rules that allow unrestricted access to AWS. resources.

text: Which database service should you use if your application and data schema require 'joins' or complex transactions?
explanation: Amazon RDS is a relational database service that supports complex transactions and JOINS, making it suitable for applications with intricate data schemas. DocumentDB is a MongoDB-compatible database, and DynamoDB is a NoSQL database that does not support JOINS.

text: Which of the following makes it easier for you to categorize, manage and filter your resources?
explanation: AWS tagging allows you to assign metadata to your AWS resources, making it easier to categorize, manage, filter, and track them for cost allocation, organization, and automation.

text: What should you consider when storing data in Amazon Glacier?
explanation: Amazon S3 Glacier is designed for data archiving and long-term backup, and retrieval times can range from minutes to hours, meaning it does not provide immediate access to data.

answers.text: Amazon Glacier only accepts data in a compressed format.
Glacier can only be used to store frequently accessed data and data archives.
Amazon Glacier does not provide immediate retrieval of data.
Attach Glacier to an EC2 Instance to be able to store data.

text: Engineers are wasting a lot of time and effort managing batch computing software in traditional data centers. Which of the following AWS services allows them to easily run thousands of batch computing jobs?
explanation: AWS Batch is designed to easily run thousands of batch computing jobs on AWS. It provisions and manages the optimal compute environment, allowing engineers to focus on their batch processing tasks rather than infrastructure management.

text: How can you increase your application's fault-tolerance while it is being hosted in AWS?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Deploying your application across multiple Availability Zones (AZs) ensures high availability. If one AZ experiences an outage, your application can continue to run in other AZs, thereby increasing fault tolerance.

answers.text: Deploy your application across multiple EC2 instances.
Deploy your application across multiple Availability Zones.
Host your application on one powerful EC2 instance type instead of multiple smaller instances.
Deploy the underlying application resources across multiple subnets.

text: Which of the following AWS Support Plans gives you 24/7 access to Cloud Support Engineers via email & phone? (Choose TWO)
explanation: The Business and Enterprise support plans provide 24/7 access to Cloud Support Engineers via email and phone, along with other enhanced features compared to the Developer and Standard plans.

text: Which of the following requires an access key ID and a secret access key to get long-lived programmatic access to AWS resources? (Choose TWO)
explanation: An IAM user's access key ID and secret access key are used to grant long-lived programmatic access to AWS resources. The AWS account root user also uses credentials for access, though programmatic access is generally discouraged for security reasons.

text: Which of the following is a benefit of the 'Loose Coupling' architecture principle?
explanation: Loose coupling means that application components can be modified independently without impacting other parts of the system, leading to increased agility and resilience. This allows for easier updates and reduces the blast radius of changes.

answers.text: It eliminates the need for change management.
It helps AWS customers reduce Privileged Access to AWS resources.
It allows individual application components or services to be modified without affecting other components.

text: A company needs to host a big data application on AWS using EC2 instances. Which of the following AWS Storage services would they choose to automatically get high throughput to multiple compute nodes?
explanation: Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) provides a scalable, elastic file system that can be accessed by multiple EC2 instances simultaneously, offering high throughput and low latency for big data applications.

text: Which of the following Cloud Computing deployment models eliminates the need to run and maintain physical data centers?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: The Cloud computing model, also known as cloud deployment, abstracts away the need for organizations to manage their own physical data centers. This allows them to focus on their core business operations rather than infrastructure maintenance.

text: What are the benefits of the AWS Marketplace service? (Choose TWO)
explanation: AWS Marketplace offers flexible pricing options, allowing customers to choose and pay for software solutions in ways that best suit their needs. It also provides software solutions that can run on AWS or other cloud vendors, broadening compatibility and choice.

answers.text: Protects customers by performing periodic security checks on listed products.
Provides cheaper options for purchasing Amazon EC2 on-demand instances.
Provides flexible pricing options that suit most customer needs.
Provides software solutions that run on AWS or any other Cloud vendor.

text: What is the benefit of Amazon EBS volumes being automatically replicated within the same availability zone?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon EBS volumes are automatically replicated within their Availability Zone to protect against hardware failure and provide durability. This replication ensures data is available even if a hardware component fails.

text: You are planning to launch an advertising campaign over the coming weekend to promote a new digital product. It is expected that there will be heavy spikes in load during the campaign period, and you can't afford any downtime. You need additional compute resources to handle the additional load. What is the most cost-effective EC2 instance purchasing option for this job?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: On-Demand Instances are the most cost-effective option for short-term, spiky workloads where high availability is critical, as they offer flexibility and no upfront commitment.

text: Which of the following AWS services integrates with AWS Shield and AWS Web Application Firewall (AWS WAF) to protect against network and application layer DDoS attacks?
explanation: Amazon CloudFront integrates with AWS Shield and AWS WAF. CloudFront acts as a content delivery network that can filter malicious traffic and provide DDoS protection at the edge, working in conjunction with Shield and WAF.

text: Which of the following services is used when encrypting EBS volumes?
explanation: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) is used to manage encryption keys that are used to encrypt Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes.

text: The AWS account administrator of your company has been fired. With the permissions granted to him as an administrator, he was able to create multiple IAM user accounts and access keys. Additionally, you are not sure whether he has access to the AWS root account or not. What should you do immediately to protect your AWS infrastructure? (Choose TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Immediately rotate all access keys for IAM users and disable any compromised credentials to prevent unauthorized access. Additionally, secure the AWS root account by changing its credentials and enabling Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).

answers.text: Download all the attached policies in a safe place.
Delete all IAM accounts and recreate them.
Use the CloudWatch service to check all API calls that have been made in your account since the administrator was fired.
Change the email address and password of the root user account and enable MFA.

text: What is the Amazon ElastiCache service used for? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon ElastiCache provides an in-memory data store and cache service, which helps to speed up application performance by reducing database load. It is often used to improve the performance of web applications by caching frequently accessed data.

answers.text: Provide an in-memory data storage service.
Reduce delivery costs using Edge Locations.
Provide a Chef-compatible cache to speed up application response.

text: The elasticity of the AWS Cloud enables customers to save costs when compared to traditional hosting providers. What can AWS customers do to benefit from the elasticity of the AWS Cloud? (Choose TWO)
explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the number of EC2 instances to match demand, while Serverless Computing (like AWS Lambda) automatically scales compute resources based on requests. Both of these leverage elasticity to optimize costs.

answers.text: Deploy your resources across multiple Availability Zones.
Deploy your resources in another region.

text: What are some of the benefits of using On-Demand EC2 instances? (Choose TWO)
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: On-Demand EC2 instances provide flexibility to scale compute capacity up or down based on application demands. They also eliminate the need to purchase excess capacity to manage traffic spikes, as you only pay for what you use.

answers.text: They provide free capacity when testing your new applications.
They are cheaper than all other EC2 options.
They remove the need to buy 'safety net' capacity to handle periodic traffic spikes.
They only require 1-2 days for setup and configuration.
You can increase or decrease your compute capacity depending on the demands of your application.

text: Each AWS Region is composed of multiple Availability Zones. Which of the following best describes what an Availability Zone is?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: An Availability Zone (AZ) is a distinct location within an AWS Region that is physically separated from other AZs. This isolation protects against failures in other AZs, enhancing resiliency.

answers.text: It is a data center designed to be completely isolated from other data centers in the same region.
It is a collection of data centers distributed in multiple countries.
It is a logically isolated network of the AWS Cloud.
It is a distinct location within a region that is insulated from « failures in other Availability Zones.

text: AWS provides disaster recovery capability by allowing customers to deploy infrastructure into multiple [...].
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Regions are geographically separate areas where AWS has data centers. Deploying infrastructure across multiple regions enables disaster recovery by ensuring that applications remain available even if one region experiences an outage.

text: A financial services company decides to migrate one of its applications to AWS. The application deals with sensitive data, such as credit card information, and must run on a PCI-compliant environment. Which of the following is the company's responsibility when building a PCI-compliant environment in AWS? (Choose TWO)
explanation: When building a PCI-compliant environment in AWS, customers are responsible for configuring AWS services correctly to meet PCI DSS standards and for implementing policies and access controls to protect cardholder data, fulfilling their part of the shared responsibility model.

answers.text: Start the migration process immediately as all AWS services are PCI compliant.
Ensure that AWS services are configured properly to meet all PCI DSS standards.
Restrict any access to cardholder data and create a policy that addresses information security for all personnel.
Configure the underlying infrastructure of AWS services to meet all PCI DSS requirements.
Ensure that all PCI DSS physical security requirements are met.

text: What is the maximum amount of data that can be stored in S3 in a single AWS account?
explanation: Amazon S3 provides virtually unlimited storage capacity, allowing you to store as much data as you need without predefined limits.

text: Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework provides recommendations to help customers select the right compute resources based on workload requirements?
explanation: The Performance Efficiency pillar provides guidance on using compute resources efficiently, including selecting the right instance types and scaling strategies to meet workload demands.

text: Which AWS service delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to users globally with low latency and high transfer speeds?
explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a globally distributed content delivery network (CDN) service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers with low latency and high transfer speeds.

text: Which of the following steps should be taken by a customer when conducting penetration testing on AWS?
explanation: Before conducting penetration testing on AWS, customers must request and obtain approval from AWS Support to ensure the testing adheres to AWS's policies and does not negatively impact other AWS customers.

answers.text: Conduct penetration testing using Amazon Inspector, and then notify AWS support.
Request and wait for approval from the customer's internal security team, and then conduct testing.
Notify AWS support, and then conduct testing immediately.
Request and wait for approval from AWS support, and then conduct testing.

text: Which AWS Cost Management tool allows you to view the most granular data about your AWS bill?
explanation: The AWS Cost and Usage report provides the most granular data about your AWS bill, which can be delivered to an S3 bucket. This data can then be analyzed with other tools. AWS Cost Explorer and the Billing dashboard offer higher-level views, and AWS Budgets is for setting budget alerts.

text: Which element of the AWS global infrastructure consists of one or more discrete data centers each with redundant power networking and connectivity which are housed in separate facilities?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: An Availability Zone is one or more discrete data centers, each with redundant power, networking, and connectivity, housed in separate facilities.

text: How many Availability Zones should compute resources be provisioned across to achieve high availability?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Provisioning compute resources across a minimum of two Availability Zones ensures that if one zone becomes unavailable, resources in another zone can continue to operate, providing high availability.

answers.text: A minimum of four or more.

text: The AWS Cloud's multiple Regions are an example of:
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Regions are distinct geographic areas that AWS has designed to host its data centers. These multiple, geographically dispersed Regions are a core component of AWS's global infrastructure.

text: Which AWS service can be used to manually launch instances based on resource requirements?
explanation: Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) is the foundational compute service in AWS that allows users to launch virtual machines, known as instances, based on their specific resource needs like CPU, memory, and storage.

text: Which is a recommended pattern for designing a highly available architecture on AWS?
explanation: Designing for failure is a core tenet of a highly available architecture. This ensures that if a single component fails, the entire application does not go down.

answers.text: Ensure that components have low-latency network connectivity.
Run enough Amazon EC2 instances to operate at peak load.
Ensure that the application is designed to accommodate failure of any single component.
Use a monolithic application that handles all operations.

text: Which AWS characteristics make AWS cost effective for a workload with dynamic user demand? (Select TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Elasticity allows AWS resources to scale up or down automatically based on demand, ensuring you only pay for what you use. Pay-as-you-go pricing means you are billed only for the resources you consume, further optimizing costs for fluctuating workloads.

text: An administrator needs to rapidly deploy a popular IT solution and start using it immediately. Where can the administrator find assistance?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS Quick Starts are automated reference deployments provided by AWS and partners that help you deploy popular solutions on AWS quickly and easily. They are designed to accelerate cloud adoption and reduce the manual effort required for deployment.

text: What is one of the advantages of the Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Amazon RDS automates many complex database administration tasks, such as provisioning, patching, backup, and recovery, making it easier to manage relational databases.

answers.text: It simplifies relational database administration tasks.
It automatically scales databases for loads.
It enabled users to dynamically adjust CPU and RAM resources.

text: Which of the following AWS Cloud services can be used to run a customer-managed relational database?
explanation: Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers on which you can install and manage your own relational database software, offering full control. Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL database service, not a relational database.

text: A user is planning to launch two additional Amazon EC2 instances to increase availability. Which action should the user take?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: To increase availability, you should launch instances across multiple Availability Zones within a single AWS Region. This provides resiliency against failures within a single data center.

answers.text: Launch the instances across multiple Availability Zones in a single AWS Region.
Launch the instances as EC2 Reserved Instances in the same AWS Region and the same Availability Zone.
Launch the instances in multiple AWS Regions but in the same Availability Zone.
Launch the instances as EC2 Spot Instances in the same AWS Region but in different Availability Zones.

text: Which of the following can limit Amazon Storage Service (Amazon S3) bucket access to specific users?
explanation: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies are used to define permissions for users and services, allowing granular control over access to AWS resources like Amazon S3 buckets.

answers.text: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies.

text: Which AWS service allows companies to connect an Amazon VPC to an on-premises data center?
explanation: AWS VPN establishes secure, encrypted tunnels between an Amazon VPC and an on-premises data center over the internet, providing a cost-effective connectivity solution. While Amazon Direct Connect offers a dedicated private connection, AWS VPN is the service specifically designed for secure VPN connections between VPCs and on-premises environments.

text: Which AWS service of feature can be used to monitor CPU usage?
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is the AWS service used for monitoring and observability, including collecting and tracking metrics such as CPU utilization from AWS resources. AWS Config monitors resource configurations but not real-time performance metrics like CPU usage.

text: Which task is AWS responsible for in the shared responsibility model for security and compliance?
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security OF the cloud, which includes tasks like updating the firmware of the underlying hardware that Amazon EC2 instances run on. Customers are responsible for security IN the cloud, such as managing operating systems and granting access.

answers.text: Granting access to individuals and services.

text: Which of the following security-related actions are available at no cost?
explanation: AWS provides free access to valuable security resources like forums, blogs, and whitepapers. While AWS Support and Professional Services offer paid assistance, educational materials are generally available without charge.

answers.text: Contacting AWS Professional Services to request a workshop.
Attending AWS classes at a local university.

text: Which storage service can be used as a low-cost option for hosting static websites?
explanation: Amazon S3 is a low-cost object storage service that can be configured to host static websites, serving HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files directly.

text: According to the AWS shared responsibility model what is the sole responsibility of AWS?
explanation: AWS is responsible for the security OF the cloud, which includes managing and securing its global infrastructure, such as edge locations. Customers are responsible for security IN the cloud.

text: Which of the following are pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Select TWO)
explanation: The AWS Well-Architected Framework includes pillars such as the Security pillar and the Performance Efficiency pillar, which guide customers in building secure, high-performing, resilient, and efficient infrastructure.

text: Which AWS service identifies security groups that allow unrestricted access to a user's AWS resources?
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor checks for security vulnerabilities, cost optimizations, performance improvements, fault tolerance, and service limits. One of its key checks is to identify security groups that allow unrestricted inbound or outbound traffic.

text: Which design principles for cloud architecture are recommended when re-architecting a large monolithic application? (Select TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: When re-architecting a monolithic application, implementing loose coupling allows components to be developed, deployed, and scaled independently. Designing for scalability ensures the application can handle varying loads efficiently by leveraging cloud elasticity.

text: When architecting cloud applications, which of the following are a key design principle?
explanation: Implementing elasticity allows applications to automatically scale up or down based on demand, which is a key design principle for cloud applications to optimize performance and cost.

text: A company has deployed several relational databases on Amazon EC2 instances Every month the database software vendor releases new security patches that need to be applied to the databases. What is the MOST efficient way to apply the security patches?
explanation: AWS Systems Manager provides capabilities for managing your EC2 instances, including automating the patching process according to a defined schedule, which is the most efficient method for applying monthly security patches.

answers.text: Connect to each database instance on a monthly basis and download and apply the necessary security patches from the vendor.
Enable automate patching for the instances using the Amazon RDS console.
In AWS Config. configure a rule for the instances and the required patch level.
Use AWS Systems Manager to automate database patching according to a schedule.

text: Which mechanism allows developers to access AWS sendees from application code?
explanation: AWS SDKs provide application programming interfaces (APIs) that allow developers to write code to access AWS services from their applications.

text: Which AWS feature will reduce the customer's total cost of ownership (TCO)?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Elastic computing allows customers to provision resources only when needed and scale them down when not in use, directly reducing costs by avoiding over-provisioning.

text: Which of the following is a benefit of using the AWS Cloud?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: By using AWS, companies can offload the management of infrastructure, allowing them to concentrate on developing new products and services that directly contribute to revenue.

answers.text: Permissive security removes the administrative burden.
Ability to focus on revenue-generating activities.
Choice of specific cloud hardware vendors.

text: Which of the following are categories of AWS Trusted Advisor? (Select TWO)
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor checks the following categories: Cost Optimization, Performance, Security, Fault Tolerance, and Service Limits. Fault Tolerance and Performance are two of these categories.

text: What is Amazon CloudWatch?
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and observability service that collects metrics, logs, and events from your AWS resources. It allows you to set alarms based on thresholds and receive notifications.

answers.text: A code repository with customizable build and team commit features.
A metrics repository with customizable notification thresholds and channels.
A security configuration repository with threat analytics.
A rule repository of a web application firewall with automated vulnerability prevention features.

text: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, which of the following activities are the customer's responsibility? (Select TWO)
explanation: Customers are responsible for client-side data encryption and configuring network access control lists (ACLs) as part of the AWS shared responsibility model. AWS manages the physical infrastructure and the operating system of managed services like RDS, but customers are responsible for OS-level patching and network security configurations.

answers.text: Patching operating system components for Amazon Relational Database Server (Amazon RDS).
Maintaining environmental controls within a data center.

text: Under the shared responsibility model, which of the following is a shared control between a customer and AWS?
explanation: An AWS shared control is a control that is managed by both AWS and the customer. Patch management is an example of a shared control, where AWS patches the underlying infrastructure, and the customer patches their operating systems and applications.

text: Which AWS service is used to pay AWS bills, and monitor usage and budget costs?
explanation: AWS Billing and Cost Management is the comprehensive suite of services for managing your AWS costs, including paying bills, monitoring usage, and setting budgets.

text: How do customers benefit from Amazon's massive economies of scale?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Amazon's massive economies of scale allow for periodic price reductions as operational efficiencies are passed on to customers. While other options relate to AWS benefits, they are not direct consequences of economies of scale benefiting customers financially.

answers.text: Periodic price reductions as the result of Amazon's operational efficiencies.
New Amazon EC2 instance types providing the latest hardware.
The ability to scale up and down when needed.
Increased reliability in the underlying hardware of Amazon EC2 instances.

text: Which AWS feature allows a company to take advantage of usage tiers for services across multiple member accounts?
explanation: Consolidated billing allows you to combine the billing for multiple AWS accounts into a single bill, and take advantage of tiered pricing for services across all accounts.

text: Which AWS services provide a way to extend an on-premises architecture to the AWS cloud? (Select TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Direct Connect allows you to establish a dedicated network connection from your premises to AWS, and AWS Storage Gateway provides a hybrid storage service that enables on-premises applications to seamlessly use AWS cloud storage.

text: Which of the following services will automatically scale with an expected increase in web traffic?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Elastic Load Balancing automatically distributes incoming web traffic across multiple targets, such as Amazon EC2 instances, and can scale to handle increased traffic.

text: Which service provides a virtually unlimited amount of online highly durable object storage?
explanation: Amazon S3 is an object storage service that offers industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance. It is designed for virtually unlimited amounts of data.

text: Which AWS feature should a customer leverage to achieve high availability of an application?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Availability Zones are distinct physical locations within an AWS Region that are engineered to be isolated from failures in other Availability Zones. By deploying applications across multiple Availability Zones, customers can achieve high availability, as the failure of one zone will not impact the availability of their application.

text: Which AWS service or feature can enhance network security by blocking requests from a particular network for a web application on AWS? (Select TWO)
explanation: AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) and Network Access Control Lists (Network ACLs) are both used to filter traffic and can block requests from specific networks to protect web applications.

text: Which of the following is a cloud architectural design principle?
explanation: Loosely coupling components in a cloud architecture enhances resilience and scalability by allowing individual components to be updated, scaled, or replaced independently.

text: Which service enables risk auditing by continuously monitoring and logging account activity, including user actions in the AWS Management Console and AWS SDKs?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail continuously monitors and logs account activity, fulfilling the requirement for risk auditing and tracking user actions.

text: Where can AWS compliance and certification reports be downloaded?
explanation: AWS Artifact is a centralized repository where customers can download AWS compliance and certification reports. This service provides on-demand access to these critical documents.

text: The financial benefits of using AWS are: (Select TWO)
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS offers reduced Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) by providing pay-as-you-go pricing, eliminating the need for upfront hardware investments. Additionally, AWS offers flexible payment options, such as deferred payment plans, to help startups manage their cash flow.

text: Which AWS service can serve a static website?
explanation: Amazon S3 can be configured to host static websites. It allows you to store your website's files and serve them directly to end-users.

text: What are the benefits of using the AWS Cloud for companies with customers in many countries around the world (Select TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Deploying applications in multiple AWS Regions and using Amazon CloudFront's edge locations globally minimizes the physical distance between users and applications, thereby reducing latency and improving the user experience.

answers.text: Companies can deploy applications in multiple AWS Regions to reduce latency.
Amazon Translate automatically translates third-party website interfaces into multiple languages.
Amazon CloudFront has multiple edge locations around the world to reduce latency.
Amazon Comprehend allows users to build applications that can respond to user requests in many languages.
Elastic Load Balancing can distribute application web traffic to multiple AWS Regions around the world which reduces latency.

text: Which of the following are main components of the AWS global infrastructure? (Select TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Regions and Availability Zones are the fundamental building blocks of AWS global infrastructure, providing geographic separation and fault tolerance for applications and data.

text: What is the AWS customer responsible for according to the AWS shared responsibility model?
explanation: In the AWS shared responsibility model, customers are responsible for data encryption and access control, while AWS handles the security of the cloud infrastructure itself.

text: If each department within a company has its own AWS account, what is one way to enable consolidated billing?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Organizations allows you to consolidate billing for multiple AWS accounts under a single payer account. By creating an organization and inviting other accounts, you can enable consolidated billing.

answers.text: Use AWS Budgets on each account to pay only to budget.
Contact AWS Support for a monthly bill.
Create an AWS Organization from the payer account and invite the other accounts to join.
Put all invoices into one Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) bucket, load data into Amazon Redshift, and then run a billing report.

text: What costs are included when comparing AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) with on-premises TCO?
explanation: AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) comparisons aim to include all relevant costs for both cloud and on-premises environments. In this case, data center security is a direct cost that would be incurred on-premises but is handled by AWS, making it a factor in the TCO comparison.

text: What is the benefit of using AWS managed services, such as Amazon ElastiCache and Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS managed services like Amazon ElastiCache and Amazon RDS abstract away operational burdens such as patching and updating the underlying operating systems, allowing customers to focus on their applications.

answers.text: They require the customer to monitor and replace failing instances.
They have better performance than customer-managed services.
They simplify patching and updating underlying OSs.
They do not require the customer to optimize instance type or size selections.

text: Which services can be used across hybrid AWS Cloud architectures? (Select TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon Route 53 provides DNS services that can be used to direct traffic to both AWS resources and on-premises resources in a hybrid architecture. Virtual Private Gateway (VGW) is a component used to establish a VPN connection between an on-premises network and an AWS VPC, enabling hybrid connectivity.

text: Which statement best describes Elastic Load Balancing?
explanation: Elastic Load Balancing automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple Amazon EC2 instances, improving application availability and fault tolerance.

answers.text: It translates a domain name into an IP address using DNC.
It distributes incoming application traffic across one or more Amazon EC2 instances.
It collects metrics on connected Amazon EC2 instances.
It automatically adjusts the number of Amazon EC2 instances to support incoming traffic.

text: Which of the following is a fast and reliable NoSQL database service?
explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed, proprietary NoSQL database service that supports key-value and document data structures. It is known for its fast performance and reliability.

text: Which AWS service would you use to obtain compliance reports and certificates?
explanation: AWS Artifact provides on-demand download of AWS compliance reports and certain contracts. It is a self-service portal that gives you access to the reports and agreements you need to assure compliance with AWS standards.

text: Which AWS services are defined as global instead of regional? (Select TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon Route 53 and Amazon CloudFront are global services, meaning they operate across all AWS Regions and are not confined to a specific geographic area.

text: How would an AWS customer easily apply common access controls to a large set of users?
explanation: Applying an IAM policy to an IAM group allows you to manage access for multiple users collectively. When you add or remove users from the group, they inherit the permissions defined in the group's policy, simplifying access control for large sets of users.

answers.text: Apply an IAM policy to an IAM group.
Apply an IAM policy to an IAM role.
Apply the same IAM policy to all IAM users with access to the same workload.
Apply an IAM policy to an Amazon Cognito user pool.

text: Which of the following is an important architectural design principle when designing cloud applications?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Designing applications to use multiple Availability Zones (AZs) is a key principle for building fault-tolerant and highly available systems in the AWS Cloud. This helps ensure that applications can withstand failures in a single data center.

text: Which service allows a company with multiple AWS accounts to combine its usage to obtain volume discounts?
explanation: AWS Organizations enables you to consolidate multiple AWS accounts and combine their usage to take advantage of volume discounts. AWS Budgets is used for setting spending limits and monitoring costs.

text: Which of the following can an AWS customer use to launch a new Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) cluster? (Select TWO)
explanation: Customers can launch new Amazon RDS clusters using the AWS Management Console for interactive provisioning or AWS CloudFormation for infrastructure as code, enabling automated and repeatable deployments.

text: Which of the following Reserved Instance (RI) pricing models provides the highest average savings compared to On-Demand pricing?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Three-year, All Upfront, Standard Reserved Instances offer the highest average savings because they commit to the longest term and the largest upfront payment, resulting in a lower effective hourly rate.

answers.text: One-year, No Upfront, Standard RI pricing.
One-year, All Upfront, Convertible RI pricing.
Three-year, All Upfront, Standard RI pricing.
Three-year, No Upfront, Convertible RI pricing.

text: Which of the following are features of Amazon CloudWatch Logs? (Select TWO)
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch Logs provides real-time monitoring of logs and allows for adjustable retention periods for log data.

answers.text: Summaries by Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS).

text: Which of the following is an AWS-managed compute service?
explanation: AWS Lambda is a serverless, event-driven compute service that runs code in response to events and automatically manages the underlying compute resources. Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers, and Amazon Aurora is a database service.

text: A company wants to reduce the physical compute footprint that developers use to run code. Which service would meet that need by enabling serverless architectures?
explanation: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that runs code in response to events and automatically manages the underlying infrastructure, thus reducing the physical compute footprint. Amazon EC2 requires managing virtual servers.

text: Which of the following is the customer's responsibility under the AWS shared responsibility model?
explanation: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, customers are responsible for patching operating systems and applications on their EC2 instances. AWS is responsible for the security of the cloud itself, including the underlying infrastructure and physical security.

text: According to the AWS shared responsibility model who is responsible for configuration management?
explanation: Configuration management in the AWS shared responsibility model is a shared responsibility. AWS is responsible for the security of the cloud, which includes the underlying infrastructure, while customers are responsible for security in the cloud, including the configuration of their deployed resources.

answers.text: It is solely the responsibility of the customer.
It is solely the responsibility of AWS.
It is shared between AWS and the customer.
It is not part of the AWS shared responsibility model.

text: Which security service automatically recognizes and classifies sensitive data or intellectual property on AWS?
explanation: Amazon Macie is a data security service that uses machine learning to discover, classify, and protect sensitive data stored in Amazon S3.

text: Which of the following BEST describe the AWS pricing model? (Select TWO)
explanation: AWS offers a pay-as-you-go model, meaning you only pay for the computing power you actually consume. Additionally, costs can be variable based on usage, as services scale up or down.

text: Under the shared responsibility model, which of the following tasks are the responsibility of the AWS customer? (Select TWO)
explanation: Customers are responsible for encrypting their application data at rest and for training their users on the secure use of AWS services. AWS is responsible for the security *of* the cloud, while customers are responsible for security *in* the cloud.

answers.text: Ensuring that application data is encrypted at rest.
Ensuring that AWS NTP servers are set to the correct time.
Ensuring that users have received security training in the use of AWS services.
Ensuring that access to data centers is restricted.
Ensuring that hardware is disposed of properly.

text: A customer is using multiple AWS accounts with separate billing. How can the customer take advantage of volume discounts with minimal impact to the AWS resources?
explanation: AWS Organizations allows you to consolidate billing from multiple AWS accounts into a single bill. This enables you to take advantage of volume discounts as your combined usage across all accounts qualifies for them.

answers.text: Create one global AWS account and move all AWS resources to tha account.
Sign up for three years of Reserved Instance pricing up front.
Use the consolidated billing feature from AWS Organizations.
Sign up for the AWS Enterprise support plan to get volume discounts.

text: Which Amazon EC2 pricing model offers the MOST significant discount when compared to OnDemand Instances?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: All Upfront Reserved Instances for a 3-year term provide the largest discount compared to On-Demand Instances because you pay the full term cost upfront, committing to a longer duration.

answers.text: A Partial Upfront Reserved Instances for a 1-year term.
All Upfront Reserved instances for a 1 year form.
All Upfront Reserved Instances for a 3 year term.
No Upfront Reserved Instances for a 3 year term.

text: Which AWS services should be used for read/write of constantly changing data? (Select TWO)
explanation: Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service that supports read/write operations for structured, constantly changing data. Amazon EFS is a scalable file storage service that supports read/write operations for constantly changing data.

text: Which AWS service allows users to identify the changes made to a resource over time?
explanation: AWS Config continuously monitors and records AWS resource configurations and allows you to automate the evaluation of recorded configurations against desired configurations. This enables you to identify changes to resources over time.

text: According to best practices, how should an application be designed to run in the AWS Cloud?
explanation: Applications should be designed using loosely coupled components to improve resilience and scalability, a key principle of the AWS Well-Architected Framework.

text: Which benefits are included with the AWS Business Support plan? (Select TWO)
explanation: The AWS Business Support plan includes 24/7 access to AWS Support via live chat or phone, as well as an unlimited number of technical support cases and contacts. Other benefits like a dedicated Technical Account Manager or annual operational reviews are included in higher-tier plans.

answers.text: 24/7 assistance by way of live chat or a telephone call.
Support from a dedicated AWS Technical Account Manager.
An unlimited number of cases and contacts.
15-minute response time for production system interruption cases.
Annual operational reviews with AWS Solutions Architects.

text: Which of the following is an AWS managed Domain Name System (DNS) web service?
explanation: Amazon Route 53 is a highly available and scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service that translates or converts, human-readable names like www.example.com into the numeric IP addresses like 192.0.2.1 that computers use to connect to each other.

text: A user must meet compliance and software licensing requirements that state a workload must be hosted on a physical server. When Amazon EC2 instance pricing option will meet these requirements?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: AWS Dedicated Hosts provide you with a physical server dedicated to your use. This meets compliance and licensing requirements that mandate specific hardware, as you have exclusive access to the underlying physical server.

text: Which of the Reserved Instance (RI) pricing models can change the attributes of the RI as long as the exchange results in the creation of RIs of equal or greater value?
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Convertible Reserved Instances allow you to exchange your RI for new RIs with different attributes as long as the new RI's attributes are of equal or greater value. This offers flexibility in adapting to changing compute needs.

text: Which service is best for storing common database query results, which helps to alleviate database access load?
explanation: Amazon ElastiCache is a fully managed in-memory caching service that can store common database query results to reduce the load on your database. Amazon Machine Learning is for machine learning, Amazon SQS is for messaging, and EC2 Instance Store is for temporary block storage.

text: When should a company consider using Amazon EC2 Spot Instances? (Select TWO)
concepts: Amazon EC2 instance types (for example, Reserved Instances, On-Demand Instances, Spot Instances)
explanation: Amazon EC2 Spot Instances are ideal for fault-tolerant, flexible applications or non-production workloads because they can be interrupted with a two-minute warning. They are not suitable for stateful workloads or applications that cannot tolerate interruptions.

answers.text: For applications that cannot have interruptions.

text: Which AWS tools assist with estimating costs? (Select three)
explanation: AWS Cost Allocation Tags, the AWS Simple Monthly Calculator, and the AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator are all services that help estimate AWS costs. The detailed billing report shows past costs, not estimates, and Cost Estimator is not a recognized AWS tool for cost estimation.

answers.text: AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator.

text: A company wants to focus on business activities instead of managing compute and capacity. Which AWS service can be used to automatically add or remove Amazon EC2 instances based on demand?
explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling enables you to automatically add or remove Amazon EC2 instances based on the demands of your applications, allowing you to maintain performance and availability while minimizing costs.

text: Which is the minimum AWS Support plan that includes Infrastructure Event Management without additional costs?
explanation: The Enterprise Support plan is the minimum AWS Support plan that includes Infrastructure Event Management without additional costs, providing proactive event coordination and dedicated support.

text: Access keys in AWS Identity and Access Management (IM1) are used to:
explanation: Access keys (access key ID and secret access key) are used to make programmatic calls to AWS services, such as through AWS APIs or the AWS CLI. They are not used for logging into the AWS Management Console, Amazon EC2 instances directly, or authenticating to AWS CodeCommit repositories.

answers.text: Log in to the AWS Management Console.
Make programmatic calls to AWS from AWS APIs.
Log in to Amazon EC2 instances.

text: Which AWS service can be used to query stored datasets directly from Amazon S3 using standard SQL?
explanation: Amazon Athena is a serverless interactive query service that enables you to analyze data directly in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. It is designed for ad-hoc analysis and does not require you to load data into a separate data warehouse.

text: How does AWS shorten the time to provision IT resources?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: AWS allows for the programmatic provisioning of resources through APIs and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like AWS CloudFormation, which significantly speeds up resource deployment compared to manual processes.

answers.text: It supplies an online IT ticketing platform for resource requests.
It supports automatic code validation services.
It provides the ability to programmatically provision existing resources.
It automates the resource request process from a company's IT vendor list.

text: Which AWS services can be used to gather information about AWS account activity? (Select TWO)
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS CloudTrail records API calls and related events in your AWS account, providing a history of actions taken. Amazon CloudWatch collects and tracks metrics, collects and monitors log files, and sets alarms, which can include account activity information where configured.

text: Which of the following are characteristics of Amazon S3? (Select TWO)
explanation: Amazon S3 is an object store, meaning data is stored as objects within buckets. It is designed to be highly durable, ensuring data is protected against failures.

text: A user wants guidance on possible savings when migrating from on-premises to AWS. Which tool is suitable for this scenario?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: The AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator helps estimate cost savings when migrating from on-premises infrastructure to AWS by comparing current costs with projected AWS costs.

answers.text: AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator.

text: Which of the following services is in the category of AWS serverless platform?
explanation: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that lets you run code without provisioning or managing servers. You pay only for the compute time you consume.

text: The use of what AWS feature or service allows companies to track and categorize spending on a detailed level?
explanation: Cost allocation tags allow you to assign metadata to your AWS resources, enabling detailed tracking and categorization of your spending for better cost management and analysis.

text: Which of the following inspects AWS environments to find opportunities that can save money for users and also improve system performance?
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor is a service that inspects your AWS environment and provides recommendations to optimize costs, improve performance, increase security, and maintain fault tolerance.

text: Web servers running on Amazon EC2 access a legacy application running in a corporate data center. What term would describe this model?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: A hybrid architecture combines on-premises infrastructure with cloud resources, allowing web servers on EC2 to connect to a legacy application in a data center.

text: What technology enables compute capacity to adjust as loads change?
explanation: AWS Auto Scaling allows you to automatically adjust the number of Amazon EC2 instances in response to changing demand. This ensures your application has the right amount of compute capacity at all times.

text: Which AWS service is a managed NoSQL database?
explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed, serverless, key-value and document database that delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale. Amazon Redshift is a data warehouse, Amazon Aurora is a relational database, and Amazon RDS offers managed relational databases.

text: Which of the following is a correct relationship between regions, Availability Zones, and edge locations?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: AWS Regions are large geographical areas, and each Region contains multiple, isolated Availability Zones. Edge locations are primarily used for content delivery via Amazon CloudFront, not for hosting core services like compute or databases.

text: What approach to transcoding a large number of individual video files adheres to AWS architecture principles?
explanation: Leveraging multiple EC2 instances in parallel allows for distributed processing, which is a fundamental principle of scalable and fault-tolerant AWS architectures for handling large workloads.

answers.text: Using a single large instance during off-peak hours.
Using a large GPU instance type.

text: Which AWS services can host a Microsoft SQL Server database? (Select TWO)
explanation: Amazon EC2 allows you to launch virtual servers where you can install and manage SQL Server. Amazon RDS provides managed SQL Server instances, simplifying database administration.

text: Which AWS IAM feature allows developers to access AWS services through the AWS CLI?
explanation: Access keys, consisting of an Access Key ID and Secret Access Key, are used to programmatically sign requests to AWS services, enabling access via tools like the AWS CLI.

text: The user is fully responsible for which action when running workloads on AWS?
explanation: AWS follows a shared responsibility model where the customer is responsible for patching their operating systems and applications, including the infrastructure they manage. AWS is responsible for the security OF the cloud, while the customer is responsible for security IN the cloud.

answers.text: Implementing controls to route application traffic.

text: Which AWS support plan includes a dedicated Technical Account Manager?
explanation: The Enterprise support plan is the only plan that provides customers with a dedicated Technical Account Manager (TAM) to help manage and optimize their AWS environment.

text: What time-savings advantage is offered with the use of Amazon Rekognition?
explanation: Amazon Rekognition offers time savings by automatically detecting objects and scenes within images, eliminating the need for manual human effort in image analysis.

answers.text: Amazon Rekognition provides automatic watermarking of images.
Amazon Rekognition provides automatic detection of objects appearing in pictures.
Amazon Recognition provides the ability to resize millions of images automatically.
Amazon Rekognition uses Amazon Mechanical Turk to allow humans to bid on object detection jobs.

text: Which AWS service can be used to automatically scale an application up and down without making capacity planning decisions?
explanation: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that automatically scales your application by running your code in response to events. You do not need to provision or manage servers, and you pay only for the compute time you consume.

text: Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) offers which of the following benefits over traditional database management?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Amazon RDS automates tasks like operating system maintenance, patching, and backups, freeing users from these operational burdens.

answers.text: AWS manages the data stored in Amazon RDS tables.
AWS manages the maintenance of the operating system.
AWS automatically scales up instance types on demand.

text: A company's web application currently has light dependencies on underlying components so when one component fails the entire web application fails. Applying which AWS Cloud design principle will address the current design issue?
explanation: Decoupling components isolates them, allowing individual components to function even if others fail, which directly addresses the issue of a single component failure bringing down the entire application.

answers.text: Implementing elasticity enabling the application to scale up or scale down as demand changes.
Enabling several EC2 instances to run in parallel to achieve better performance.
Focusing on decoupling components by isolating them and ensuring individual components can function when other components.
Doubling EC2 computing resources to increase system fault tolerance.

text: A customer would like to design and build a new workload on AWS Cloud but does not have the AWS-related software technical expertise in-house. Which of the following AWS programs can a customer take advantage of to achieve that outcome?
explanation: AWS Partner Network (APN) Consulting Partners are companies that help customers design, build, migrate, and manage their workloads and applications on AWS. They offer specialized expertise and services to address skill gaps within an organization.

text: Which service stores objects, provides real-time access to those objects, and offers versioning and lifecycle capabilities?
explanation: Amazon S3 is a highly scalable object storage service offering real-time access, versioning, and lifecycle management capabilities for objects.

text: Distributing workloads across multiple Availability Zones supports which cloud architecture design principle?
concepts: AWS global infrastructure (for example, AWS Regions, Availability Zones)
explanation: Distributing workloads across multiple Availability Zones is a key strategy to ensure business continuity by preparing for the failure of a single Availability Zone. This aligns with the principle of designing for failure.

text: Which service should a customer use to consolidate and centrally manage multiple AWS accounts?
explanation: AWS Organizations is the service that allows customers to consolidate and centrally manage multiple AWS accounts. It enables policy-based management and offers features like account creation, grouping, and access control.

text: How can a company reduce its Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) using AWS?
concepts: Benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud
explanation: Migrating to AWS allows companies to shift from capital expenditures (like purchasing hardware) to operational expenditures, which can be more cost-effective and reduce TCO. It also provides tools to optimize spending.

answers.text: By having no responsibility for third-party license costs.

text: Which options does AWS make available for customers who want to learn about security in the cloud in an instructor-led setting? (Select TWO)
explanation: AWS offers instructor-led learning through AWS Classroom Training for in-depth skill development. AWS Online Tech Talks also provide live, interactive sessions on various AWS topics, including security.

text: Which of the following will enhance the security of access to the AWS Management Console'? (Select TWO)
explanation: AWS Multi-Factor Authentication (AWS MFA) and password policies are key security measures that enhance the security of access to the AWS Management Console by adding layers of authentication and control over user credentials.
